PROJECT 45: E-Commerce Platform — Full Architecture

Problem: Design a COMPLETE Data Platform for a Global E-Commerce Company

🎯 THE HYPOTHETICAL PROBLEM

SCENARIO:
    You are the Lead Data Architect at "NovaMart"
    — a rapidly growing global e-commerce company

    BUSINESS REQUIREMENTS:
    ├── 15 million registered users across US, EU, Asia
    ├── 2 million products with wildly varying attributes
    ├── 50,000 orders per hour at peak (Black Friday: 500,000/hr)
    ├── Product search with autocomplete, fuzzy matching, faceted filters
    ├── Real-time inventory tracking across 12 warehouses
    ├── Shopping cart that NEVER loses items (even on server crash)
    ├── Personalized recommendations based on browsing + purchase history
    ├── Fraud detection within 200ms of payment submission
    ├── Business analytics: revenue, cohort analysis, demand forecasting
    ├── GDPR compliance: EU user data stays in EU
    ├── 99.99% uptime SLA for customer-facing operations
    └── Sub-50ms response time for product pages globally

    THE QUESTION:
    → Which database handles WHICH part of this system?
    → Where does MongoDB shine?
    → Where does Redshift shine?
    → Where do they work TOGETHER?
    → How does Kafka connect them?
    → What about search, caching, ML pipelines?

    THIS PROJECT IS THE CONVERGENCE OF EVERYTHING:
    Projects 1-5   → CRUD fundamentals
    Projects 6-8   → Document modeling
    Projects 9-11  → Indexing strategies
    Projects 12-13 → Aggregation pipelines
    Project 14     → Embedding vs referencing decisions
    Project 15     → OLTP vs OLAP workload separation
    Projects 16-20 → Schema design patterns
    Projects 21-22 → Sharding and shard key selection
    Projects 23-24 → Replica sets and consistency tuning
    Projects 25-26 → Change streams and triggers
    Projects 27-28 → Transactions
    Project 29     → Atlas Search
    Project 30     → Time Series collections
    Projects 31-33 → Performance optimization
    Projects 34-36 → Hybrid architecture and data federation
    Projects 37-38 → Security and encryption
    Projects 39-40 → Zone sharding and resharding
    Project 41     → Global clusters
    Project 42     → Real-time analytics
    Project 43     → Event sourcing
    Project 44     → Kafka integration

🔴 REDSHIFT MINDSET — How You Would Solve This Entire Platform

The Redshift-Only Architecture (What Many Analytics Teams Attempt)

YOUR INSTINCT (trained by Redshift thinking):

    "Everything goes into Redshift. One warehouse to rule them all."

    ATTEMPT:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │                    REDSHIFT CLUSTER                          │
    │                                                              │
    │  users table              → 15M rows, normalized             │
    │  products table           → 2M rows, wide sparse columns     │
    │  product_attributes table → key-value EAV pattern            │
    │  orders table             → billions of rows, partitioned     │
    │  order_items table        → JOIN to orders                    │
    │  carts table              → temporary session data            │
    │  cart_items table         → JOIN to carts                     │
    │  inventory table          → 12 warehouse × 2M products       │
    │  user_activity table      → clickstream, billions of rows     │
    │  search_log table         → every search query logged         │
    │  payments table           → financial transactions            │
    │  recommendations table    → precomputed per user              │
    │  addresses table          → multiple per user                 │
    │  reviews table            → ratings + text per product        │
    │                                                              │
    │  Distribution: orders by user_id, products by category_id    │
    │  Sort keys: most tables sorted by timestamp                  │
    │  Materialized views: revenue dashboards, cohort tables       │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    SQL FOR A SINGLE PRODUCT PAGE:
    SELECT p.*, pa.attr_key, pa.attr_value,
           r.avg_rating, r.review_count,
           i.stock_qty, i.warehouse_id
    FROM products p
    LEFT JOIN product_attributes pa ON p.product_id = pa.product_id
    LEFT JOIN (SELECT product_id, AVG(rating) avg_rating, COUNT(*) review_count
               FROM reviews GROUP BY product_id) r ON p.product_id = r.product_id
    LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.product_id = i.product_id
    WHERE p.product_id = 12345;

    → 4-way JOIN for ONE product page
    → Redshift query latency: 200ms-2s (columnar scan overhead)
    → At 50,000 concurrent users: WLM queue saturation
    → Cart operations: UPDATE with BEGIN/COMMIT per click → terrible concurrency

Where Redshift-Only Architecture BREAKS

FAILURE POINTS:

1. PRODUCT PAGE LATENCY
   → Redshift minimum query latency: ~100-500ms (even cached)
   → Requirement: <50ms globally
   → Redshift cannot serve real-time product page APIs

2. SHOPPING CART CONCURRENCY
   → 50,000 users modifying carts simultaneously
   → Each cart change = UPDATE + transaction
   → Redshift WLM: ~50 concurrent queries max (even with concurrency scaling)
   → Cart would freeze under load

3. PRODUCT CATALOG FLEXIBILITY
   → Electronics: voltage, wattage, connectivity
   → Clothing: size, color, fabric, gender
   → Books: ISBN, pages, author, publisher
   → Food: calories, allergens, expiry_date
   → Redshift: one wide table with 200 NULLable columns
     OR EAV pattern (entity-attribute-value) → terrible query performance

4. REAL-TIME INVENTORY
   → Stock changes 1000x/second across 12 warehouses
   → Redshift: not designed for high-frequency single-row updates
   → Latency for inventory check: seconds, not milliseconds

5. SEARCH
   → "blue runnng shoes size 10" (typo + filters)
   → Redshift: LIKE '%blue%' AND LIKE '%running%' → full table scan
   → No fuzzy matching, no relevance scoring, no autocomplete

6. FRAUD DETECTION
   → Must analyze payment within 200ms
   → Redshift: query compilation alone takes 200ms+
   → Cannot serve real-time decision making

CONCLUSION:
    Redshift is ESSENTIAL for this platform — but only for PART of it
    You need MongoDB for the operational layer
    You need Kafka for the event backbone
    You need both databases working TOGETHER

🟢 MONGODB + REDSHIFT — The Correct Architecture

The Complete System Design

                        ┌──────────────────────────────┐
                        │       GLOBAL CDN              │
                        │   (CloudFront / Fastly)       │
                        └──────────┬───────────────────┘
                                   │
                        ┌──────────▼───────────────────┐
                        │     API GATEWAY               │
                        │  (Kong / AWS API Gateway)     │
                        └──────────┬───────────────────┘
                                   │
                 ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
                 │                 │                      │
        ┌────────▼──────┐  ┌──────▼───────┐  ┌──────────▼────────┐
        │  Product       │  │  Cart         │  │  Order             │
        │  Service       │  │  Service      │  │  Service           │
        │  (Node.js)     │  │  (Node.js)    │  │  (Java/Python)     │
        └────────┬──────┘  └──────┬───────┘  └──────────┬────────┘
                 │                │                      │
    ┌────────────┼────────────────┼──────────────────────┼────────────┐
    │            │                │                      │            │
    │   ┌────────▼──────┐  ┌─────▼────────┐  ┌─────────▼────────┐   │
    │   │  MongoDB       │  │  MongoDB      │  │  MongoDB          │   │
    │   │  Atlas         │  │  Atlas        │  │  Atlas            │   │
    │   │                │  │               │  │                   │   │
    │   │ • products     │  │ • carts       │  │ • orders          │   │
    │   │ • categories   │  │               │  │ • payments        │   │
    │   │ • reviews      │  │ (TTL expiry)  │  │ • shipments       │   │
    │   │ • inventory    │  │               │  │                   │   │
    │   │                │  │               │  │ (Multi-doc ACID   │   │
    │   │ (Atlas Search  │  │ (Sub-ms       │  │  transactions)    │   │
    │   │  for product   │  │  read/write)  │  │                   │   │
    │   │  search)       │  │               │  │                   │   │
    │   └────────┬──────┘  └──────────────┘  └─────────┬────────┘   │
    │            │                                      │            │
    │   OPERATIONAL LAYER (MongoDB Atlas Global Cluster)             │
    └────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼────────────┘
                 │                                      │
                 │         ┌─────────────────┐          │
                 │         │                 │          │
                 ├────────►│  KAFKA CLUSTER  │◄─────────┤
                 │         │  (MSK / Atlas   │          │
                 │ Change   │   Stream        │  Change  │
                 │ Streams  │   Processing)   │  Streams │
                 │         │                 │          │
                 │         └───────┬─────────┘          │
                 │                 │                     │
                 │    ┌────────────┼──────────────┐      │
                 │    │            │              │      │
                 │    ▼            ▼              ▼      │
                 │ ┌──────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌──────────┐  │
                 │ │Redshift│ │ S3 Data │  │ ML       │  │
                 │ │       │  │ Lake    │  │ Pipeline │  │
                 │ │Revenue│  │         │  │          │  │
                 │ │Cohorts│  │Raw JSON │  │Recommend.│  │
                 │ │Demand │  │Parquet  │  │Fraud Det.│  │
                 │ │Trends │  │Archive  │  │Forecasts │  │
                 │ └───┬───┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬─────┘  │
                 │     │           │             │        │
                 │     ▼           ▼             ▼        │
                 │  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐   │
                 │  │       ANALYTICS & BI LAYER       │   │
                 │  │  QuickSight + MongoDB Charts     │   │
                 │  │  + Tableau + Custom Dashboards    │   │
                 │  └─────────────────────────────────┘   │
                 │                                        │
    ANALYTICAL LAYER (Redshift + S3 + ML)                 │
    ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

🧩 COMPONENT-BY-COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS

Decision Framework: "Which Database For Which Data?"

FOR EACH COMPONENT, ASK:
    1. Is this OPERATIONAL (serving users) or ANALYTICAL (serving analysts)?
    2. What's the latency requirement? (ms vs seconds)
    3. What's the concurrency? (thousands vs dozens)
    4. Is the schema fixed or flexible?
    5. Is this read-heavy or write-heavy?
    6. Does it need JOINs across many entities?
    7. Does it need full-text search?
    8. Does it need real-time updates?

┌───────────────┬────────┬─────────┬───────────┬──────────┬────────────┐
│ COMPONENT      │LATENCY │CONCURR. │ SCHEMA    │ PATTERN  │ DATABASE   │
├───────────────┼────────┼─────────┼───────────┼──────────┼────────────┤
│ Product Catalog│ <50ms  │ 100K/s  │ Flexible  │ Read 95% │ MongoDB    │
│ Shopping Cart  │ <10ms  │ 50K/s   │ Flexible  │ R/W 50/50│ MongoDB    │
│ User Profiles  │ <20ms  │ 50K/s   │ Semi-flex │ Read 80% │ MongoDB    │
│ Orders         │ <100ms │ 5K/s    │ Structured│ Write 60%│ MongoDB    │
│ Payments       │ <200ms │ 5K/s    │ Structured│ Write 70%│ MongoDB    │
│ Inventory      │ <10ms  │ 10K/s   │ Fixed     │ R/W 50/50│ MongoDB    │
│ Product Search │ <100ms │ 20K/s   │ Flexible  │ Read 99% │ Atlas Srch │
│ Reviews        │ <50ms  │ 2K/s    │ Semi-flex │ Read 90% │ MongoDB    │
│ Clickstream    │ <500ms │ 100K/s  │ Flexible  │ Write 99%│ Kafka→S3   │
│ Revenue Reports│ <30s   │ 50      │ Fixed     │ Read 100%│ Redshift   │
│ Cohort Analysis│ <60s   │ 20      │ Fixed     │ Read 100%│ Redshift   │
│ Demand Forecast│ <120s  │ 10      │ Fixed     │ Read 100%│ Redshift   │
│ Fraud Detection│ <200ms │ 5K/s    │ Flexible  │ Read 80% │ MongoDB+ML │
│ Recommendations│ <50ms  │ 100K/s  │ Flexible  │ Read 95% │ MongoDB    │
│ Audit Trail    │ Async  │ 50K/s   │ Fixed     │ Write 99%│ Kafka→S3   │
│ Notifications  │ <1s    │ 10K/s   │ Flexible  │ Write 80%│ MongoDB    │
└───────────────┴────────┴─────────┴───────────┴──────────┴────────────┘

SCORE:
    MongoDB (operational):     11 components
    Redshift (analytical):     3 components
    Kafka + S3 (streaming):    2 components
    Atlas Search (search):     1 component (runs on MongoDB infrastructure)

THIS IS WHY:
    → MongoDB handles the APPLICATION layer
    → Redshift handles the ANALYTICS layer
    → Kafka connects them
    → Neither replaces the other

📦 COMPONENT 1: PRODUCT CATALOG

Why MongoDB (References: Projects 6, 7, 16-20, 29)

PROBLEM:
    2 million products
    → Electronics: voltage, wattage, ports, connectivity, warranty
    → Clothing: size_chart, colors, fabric, gender, season
    → Books: isbn, author, publisher, pages, edition
    → Groceries: calories, allergens, expiry_date, organic_certified
    → Each category adds 10-30 unique attributes
    → New categories added monthly (home automation, pet tech, etc.)
    → Product pages need <50ms load time globally
    → Full-text search with autocomplete and faceted filtering

REDSHIFT APPROACH (and why it fails here):
    Option A: Wide sparse table → 200+ NULLable columns → wastes storage
    Option B: EAV table → terrible query performance → 50+ JOINs
    Option C: Multiple tables per category → schema explosion
    
    ANY option: minimum 200ms query latency → fails <50ms requirement
    Search: LIKE '%keyword%' → full scan → seconds

MONGODB APPROACH (why it wins):
    → Polymorphic pattern (Project 18): one collection, varying shapes
    → Subset pattern (Project 19): embed top-10 reviews in product
    → Computed pattern (Project 20): pre-aggregated ratings
    → Atlas Search (Project 29): Lucene-powered search
    → Global cluster (Project 41): local reads in each region

Document Design

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// PRODUCT CATALOG — MongoDB Document Model
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// Pattern: Polymorphic (Project 18) + Subset (Project 19)
//        + Computed (Project 20)

// ─── ELECTRONICS PRODUCT ─────────────────────────────
{
    _id: ObjectId("prod_electronics_001"),
    
    // ── Common fields (all products) ──
    sku: "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
    name: "ProGlide Wireless Mouse",
    slug: "proglide-wireless-mouse",              // URL-friendly
    brand: "TechCo",
    category: ["Electronics", "Computer Accessories", "Mice"],
    price: {
        amount: NumberDecimal("29.99"),
        currency: "USD",
        compare_at: NumberDecimal("39.99"),        // strikethrough price
        cost: NumberDecimal("12.50")               // internal cost
    },
    status: "active",                              // active | draft | archived
    
    // ── Computed fields (Project 20) ──
    // Updated atomically via $inc on each event
    computed: {
        avg_rating: 4.3,
        review_count: 1247,
        total_sold: 15820,
        view_count_30d: 45000
    },
    
    // ── Subset pattern (Project 19) ──
    // Top 5 reviews embedded, full reviews in separate collection
    recent_reviews: [
        {
            user_name: "Alice M.",
            rating: 5,
            title: "Best mouse ever",
            snippet: "Incredibly smooth tracking and battery lasts forever...",
            date: ISODate("2024-05-28"),
            helpful_votes: 42
        },
        // ... 4 more
    ],
    
    // ── Product images ──
    images: [
        { url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-main.jpg", alt: "Front view", primary: true },
        { url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-side.jpg", alt: "Side view", primary: false },
        { url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-bottom.jpg", alt: "Bottom sensor", primary: false }
    ],
    
    // ── Polymorphic fields (ONLY for electronics) ──
    // This is what Redshift CANNOT do elegantly
    attributes: {
        connectivity: ["Bluetooth 5.0", "USB-C Dongle"],
        dpi: { min: 400, max: 16000 },
        battery: { type: "AA", life_months: 18 },
        weight_grams: 85,
        compatible_os: ["Windows", "macOS", "Linux"],
        warranty_months: 24
    },
    
    // ── Inventory summary (real-time) ──
    inventory: {
        total_available: 1523,
        warehouses: [
            { warehouse_id: "WH-US-EAST", qty: 450, reserved: 12 },
            { warehouse_id: "WH-US-WEST", qty: 380, reserved: 8 },
            { warehouse_id: "WH-EU-CENTRAL", qty: 400, reserved: 5 },
            { warehouse_id: "WH-ASIA-EAST", qty: 293, reserved: 3 }
        ]
    },
    
    // ── SEO and search ──
    search_keywords: ["wireless mouse", "bluetooth mouse", "ergonomic mouse",
                      "gaming mouse", "computer mouse"],
    
    // ── Metadata ──
    created_at: ISODate("2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"),
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-30T14:22:00Z"),
    created_by: "admin_user_001",
    version: 12
}

// ─── CLOTHING PRODUCT (DIFFERENT SHAPE, SAME COLLECTION) ──
{
    _id: ObjectId("prod_clothing_001"),
    sku: "CLTH-RS-2024-001",
    name: "TrailBlazer Running Shoes",
    slug: "trailblazer-running-shoes",
    brand: "AthletePro",
    category: ["Footwear", "Running", "Men"],
    price: {
        amount: NumberDecimal("129.99"),
        currency: "USD",
        compare_at: null,
        cost: NumberDecimal("45.00")
    },
    status: "active",
    computed: {
        avg_rating: 4.7,
        review_count: 3891,
        total_sold: 28450,
        view_count_30d: 92000
    },
    recent_reviews: [ /* ... top 5 ... */ ],
    images: [ /* ... */ ],
    
    // ── Polymorphic fields (ONLY for clothing/footwear) ──
    attributes: {
        gender: "Men",
        color_options: [
            { name: "Midnight Black", hex: "#1a1a2e", available: true },
            { name: "Ocean Blue", hex: "#0077b6", available: true },
            { name: "Sunset Red", hex: "#e63946", available: false }
        ],
        sizes: [
            { size: "7", us: "7", eu: "40", in_stock: true },
            { size: "8", us: "8", eu: "41", in_stock: true },
            { size: "9", us: "9", eu: "42", in_stock: true },
            { size: "10", us: "10", eu: "43", in_stock: true },
            { size: "11", us: "11", eu: "44", in_stock: false },
            { size: "12", us: "12", eu: "45", in_stock: true }
        ],
        material: "Mesh upper, EVA midsole, rubber outsole",
        cushioning: "Responsive foam",
        terrain: ["Road", "Light Trail"],
        drop_mm: 8,
        weight_grams: 280
    },
    
    inventory: {
        total_available: 4200,
        warehouses: [ /* per size per warehouse breakdown */ ]
    },
    
    search_keywords: ["running shoes", "trail shoes", "athletic shoes",
                      "men running shoes", "lightweight running"],
    
    created_at: ISODate("2024-02-10T10:00:00Z"),
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-29T09:15:00Z"),
    created_by: "admin_user_003",
    version: 8
}

Redshift Equivalent (Why It Struggles)

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- REDSHIFT: Attempting the same product catalog
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- Option A: Wide sparse table (ugly but works)
CREATE TABLE store.products (
    product_id      BIGINT IDENTITY(1,1) PRIMARY KEY,
    sku             VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    name            VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    slug            VARCHAR(255),
    brand           VARCHAR(100),
    price_amount    DECIMAL(10,2),
    price_currency  VARCHAR(3),
    price_compare   DECIMAL(10,2),
    price_cost      DECIMAL(10,2),
    status          VARCHAR(20),
    avg_rating      DECIMAL(3,2),
    review_count    INTEGER,
    total_sold      BIGINT,
    
    -- Electronics-specific (NULL for non-electronics)
    connectivity    VARCHAR(500),      -- JSON string? comma-separated?
    dpi_min         INTEGER,
    dpi_max         INTEGER,
    battery_type    VARCHAR(20),
    battery_life    INTEGER,
    warranty_months INTEGER,
    
    -- Clothing-specific (NULL for non-clothing)
    gender          VARCHAR(20),
    material        VARCHAR(500),
    cushioning      VARCHAR(100),
    drop_mm         INTEGER,
    
    -- Books-specific (NULL for non-books)
    isbn            VARCHAR(20),
    author          VARCHAR(255),
    pages           INTEGER,
    publisher       VARCHAR(255),
    
    -- Groceries-specific (NULL for non-groceries)
    calories        INTEGER,
    organic         BOOLEAN,
    
    -- ... 150+ more NULLable columns as categories grow
    
    created_at      TIMESTAMP DEFAULT GETDATE()
)
DISTSTYLE KEY DISTKEY(product_id) SORTKEY(created_at);

-- PROBLEMS:
-- 1. 80% of columns are NULL for any given row → wasted scans
-- 2. Adding new category = ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN (DDL lock)
-- 3. Arrays (sizes, colors, images) need separate tables
-- 4. Nested objects (price, attributes) flattened into columns
-- 5. No way to embed reviews inside product row

-- Need SEPARATE tables for arrays:
CREATE TABLE store.product_categories (
    product_id BIGINT, category_name VARCHAR(100)
);
CREATE TABLE store.product_images (
    product_id BIGINT, url VARCHAR(1000), alt_text VARCHAR(255), is_primary BOOLEAN
);
CREATE TABLE store.product_sizes (
    product_id BIGINT, size VARCHAR(10), us_size VARCHAR(10), eu_size VARCHAR(10), in_stock BOOLEAN
);
CREATE TABLE store.product_colors (
    product_id BIGINT, color_name VARCHAR(50), hex_code VARCHAR(7), available BOOLEAN
);
CREATE TABLE store.product_keywords (
    product_id BIGINT, keyword VARCHAR(100)
);
CREATE TABLE store.product_warehouse_inventory (
    product_id BIGINT, warehouse_id VARCHAR(20), qty INTEGER, reserved INTEGER
);

-- PRODUCT PAGE QUERY: 7-table JOIN
SELECT p.*, 
       pc.category_name,
       pi.url, pi.alt_text,
       ps.size, ps.in_stock,
       pw.warehouse_id, pw.qty
FROM store.products p
LEFT JOIN store.product_categories pc ON p.product_id = pc.product_id
LEFT JOIN store.product_images pi ON p.product_id = pi.product_id
LEFT JOIN store.product_sizes ps ON p.product_id = ps.product_id
LEFT JOIN store.product_warehouse_inventory pw ON p.product_id = pw.product_id
WHERE p.product_id = 12345;

-- This returns DENORMALIZED rows (cartesian product of arrays)
-- Application must reconstruct the nested structure
-- Latency: 200ms-1s depending on cluster load
-- Concurrency: limited by WLM slots

📦 COMPONENT 2: SHOPPING CART

Why MongoDB (References: Projects 4, 5, 6, 27)

PROBLEM:
    → 50,000 concurrent active carts at peak
    → Each "add to cart" = sub-10ms response required
    → Cart must survive server restarts
    → Abandoned carts auto-expire after 7 days
    → Cart → Order transition must be atomic
    → Price must be locked at checkout time

REDSHIFT FAILURE:
    → 50K writes/second → Redshift cannot handle this concurrency
    → Single-row UPDATE latency: 50-200ms → too slow
    → No TTL (auto-expiry) → need scheduled DELETE jobs
    → Transaction overhead for each cart modification → WLM saturation

MONGODB WIN:
    → Sub-ms single-document updates ($push, $pull, $set)
    → TTL index (Project 5): automatic expiry
    → Single-document atomicity (Project 27): cart is ONE document
    → Embedding (Project 6): all items inside cart document

Document Design

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SHOPPING CART — MongoDB Document Model
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

{
    _id: ObjectId("cart_001"),
    user_id: ObjectId("user_001"),         // null for anonymous carts
    session_id: "sess_abc123def456",       // for anonymous users
    status: "active",                      // active | checkout | converted | abandoned
    
    items: [
        {
            product_id: ObjectId("prod_electronics_001"),
            sku: "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
            name: "ProGlide Wireless Mouse",        // denormalized for display
            image_url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-main.jpg",
            price_at_add: NumberDecimal("29.99"),    // price locked at add time
            current_price: NumberDecimal("29.99"),   // updated periodically
            quantity: 2,
            variant: null,
            added_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:15:00Z")
        },
        {
            product_id: ObjectId("prod_clothing_001"),
            sku: "CLTH-RS-2024-001",
            name: "TrailBlazer Running Shoes",
            image_url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/rs001-main.jpg",
            price_at_add: NumberDecimal("129.99"),
            current_price: NumberDecimal("129.99"),
            quantity: 1,
            variant: { size: "10", color: "Midnight Black" },
            added_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:18:00Z")
        }
    ],
    
    // ── Computed totals (updated on each item change) ──
    subtotal: NumberDecimal("189.97"),
    item_count: 3,                          // sum of quantities
    
    // ── Applied promotions ──
    coupons: [
        { code: "SAVE10", discount_pct: 10, applied_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:20:00Z") }
    ],
    
    // ── Timestamps ──
    created_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:15:00Z"),
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:20:00Z"),
    expires_at: ISODate("2024-06-07T10:15:00Z")    // TTL: 7 days from creation
}

Cart Operations — MongoDB Code

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SHOPPING CART OPERATIONS — mongosh
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── CREATE TTL INDEX (auto-expire abandoned carts) ──
// Reference: Project 5 — TTL indexes
db.carts.createIndex(
    { "expires_at": 1 },
    { expireAfterSeconds: 0 }     // expire when expires_at is reached
)

// ─── ADD ITEM TO CART ────────────────────────────────
// Single atomic operation — no transaction needed (Project 27)
db.carts.updateOne(
    { _id: ObjectId("cart_001"), "items.product_id": { $ne: ObjectId("prod_books_001") } },
    {
        $push: {
            items: {
                product_id: ObjectId("prod_books_001"),
                sku: "BOOK-PCC-2024-001",
                name: "Python Crash Course",
                image_url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/pcc-main.jpg",
                price_at_add: NumberDecimal("35.99"),
                current_price: NumberDecimal("35.99"),
                quantity: 1,
                variant: null,
                added_at: new Date()
            }
        },
        $inc: {
            subtotal: NumberDecimal("35.99"),
            item_count: 1
        },
        $set: { updated_at: new Date() },
        $currentDate: { expires_at: false }    // don't reset TTL
    },
    { upsert: false }
)

// ─── UPDATE ITEM QUANTITY ────────────────────────────
db.carts.updateOne(
    { _id: ObjectId("cart_001"), "items.product_id": ObjectId("prod_electronics_001") },
    {
        $set: {
            "items.$.quantity": 3,
            updated_at: new Date()
        },
        $inc: {
            subtotal: NumberDecimal("29.99"),    // added 1 more
            item_count: 1
        }
    }
)

// ─── REMOVE ITEM FROM CART ───────────────────────────
db.carts.updateOne(
    { _id: ObjectId("cart_001") },
    {
        $pull: {
            items: { product_id: ObjectId("prod_books_001") }
        },
        $inc: {
            subtotal: NumberDecimal("-35.99"),
            item_count: -1
        },
        $set: { updated_at: new Date() }
    }
)

// ── REDSHIFT EQUIVALENT (for reference) ──────────────
// BEGIN;
// DELETE FROM store.cart_items
// WHERE cart_id = 'cart_001' AND product_id = 'prod_books_001';
// UPDATE store.carts
// SET subtotal = subtotal - 35.99,
//     item_count = item_count - 1,
//     updated_at = GETDATE()
// WHERE cart_id = 'cart_001';
// COMMIT;
// → Two tables, explicit transaction, 50-200ms latency
// → MongoDB: one collection, atomic update, <5ms latency

📦 COMPONENT 3: ORDER PROCESSING

Why MongoDB with Transactions (References: Projects 27, 28, 43)

PROBLEM:
    → Cart → Order conversion must be ATOMIC
    → Debit inventory + create order + create payment = all or nothing
    → Order history must be queryable by user (fast)
    → Order data feeds into Redshift for analytics (via Kafka)
    → 50,000 orders/hour at peak

WHY MONGODB (not Redshift):
    → Sub-100ms order creation
    → Multi-document transaction for cart→order conversion (Project 28)
    → Event sourcing: order state changes captured via change streams (Project 43)
    → Flexible: orders contain full snapshot of items at time of purchase
    → Serves order history API directly

WHY ALSO REDSHIFT (for analytics):
    → "Revenue per category per quarter for last 3 years"
    → "Customer cohort retention analysis"
    → "Demand forecasting by region and season"
    → These queries scan millions of orders → Redshift's strength

Document Design

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// ORDER — MongoDB Document Model
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

{
    _id: ObjectId("order_001"),
    order_number: "NM-2024-0531-78234",      // human-readable
    
    // ── Customer snapshot at order time ──
    customer: {
        user_id: ObjectId("user_001"),
        name: "Sarah Johnson",
        email: "sarah@example.com",
        tier: "Gold"
    },
    
    // ── Full item snapshot (denormalized from products) ──
    // Items are FROZEN at purchase time — prices never change retroactively
    items: [
        {
            product_id: ObjectId("prod_electronics_001"),
            sku: "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
            name: "ProGlide Wireless Mouse",
            price: NumberDecimal("29.99"),
            quantity: 2,
            subtotal: NumberDecimal("59.98"),
            variant: null,
            warehouse_id: "WH-US-EAST"           // fulfilled from this warehouse
        },
        {
            product_id: ObjectId("prod_clothing_001"),
            sku: "CLTH-RS-2024-001",
            name: "TrailBlazer Running Shoes",
            price: NumberDecimal("129.99"),
            quantity: 1,
            subtotal: NumberDecimal("129.99"),
            variant: { size: "10", color: "Midnight Black" },
            warehouse_id: "WH-US-EAST"
        }
    ],
    
    // ── Pricing ──
    pricing: {
        subtotal: NumberDecimal("189.97"),
        discount: NumberDecimal("19.00"),          // SAVE10 coupon
        tax: NumberDecimal("15.37"),
        shipping: NumberDecimal("0.00"),           // free shipping
        total: NumberDecimal("186.34")
    },
    
    // ── Shipping ──
    shipping: {
        address: {
            street: "123 Oak Lane",
            city: "Austin",
            state: "TX",
            zip: "78701",
            country: "US"
        },
        method: "standard",
        estimated_delivery: ISODate("2024-06-05"),
        tracking: null                             // filled when shipped
    },
    
    // ── Payment ──
    payment: {
        method: "credit_card",
        card_last_four: [REDACTED:CREDIT_DEBIT_NUMBER]2",
        card_brand: "Visa",
        transaction_id: "txn_ch_3abc123def456",
        status: "captured",
        captured_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:30Z")
    },
    
    // ── Order lifecycle (Event Sourcing — Project 43) ──
    status: "processing",
    status_history: [
        { status: "created",    at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:00Z"), by: "system" },
        { status: "paid",       at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:30Z"), by: "payment_service" },
        { status: "processing", at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:26:00Z"), by: "fulfillment_service" }
    ],
    
    // ── Metadata ──
    coupons_applied: ["SAVE10"],
    source: "web",                                 // web | mobile_app | api
    ip_address: "203.0.113.42",
    user_agent: "Mozilla/5.0...",
    region: "US",                                  // for zone sharding (Project 39)
    
    created_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:00Z"),
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:26:00Z")
}

Cart-to-Order Transaction — MongoDB Code

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CART → ORDER CONVERSION — Multi-Document Transaction
# Reference: Project 28 (multi-document ACID)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from pymongo import MongoClient
from datetime import datetime
from bson import ObjectId, Decimal128

client = MongoClient("mongodb+srv://admin:pass@cluster0.mongodb.net/")
db = client["ecommerce"]

def convert_cart_to_order(cart_id, payment_info):
    """
    Atomic cart → order conversion
    Steps (ALL or NOTHING):
        1. Read cart
        2. Verify inventory for all items
        3. Decrement inventory
        4. Create order document
        5. Mark cart as converted
        6. Update customer stats
    """
    
    with client.start_session() as session:
        with session.start_transaction():
            try:
                # ── Step 1: Read cart ──────────────────────
                cart = db.carts.find_one(
                    {"_id": cart_id, "status": "active"},
                    session=session
                )
                if not cart:
                    raise Exception("Cart not found or not active")
                
                # ── Step 2: Verify and decrement inventory ──
                for item in cart["items"]:
                    # Find nearest warehouse with stock
                    result = db.products.update_one(
                        {
                            "_id": item["product_id"],
                            "inventory.warehouses": {
                                "$elemMatch": {
                                    "qty": {"$gte": item["quantity"]}
                                }
                            }
                        },
                        {
                            "$inc": {
                                "inventory.total_available": -item["quantity"],
                                "inventory.warehouses.$.qty": -item["quantity"],
                                "computed.total_sold": item["quantity"]
                            }
                        },
                        session=session
                    )
                    if result.modified_count == 0:
                        raise Exception(
                            f"Insufficient inventory for {item['sku']}"
                        )
                
                # ── Step 3: Create order ───────────────────
                order_number = f"NM-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m%d')}-{ObjectId().generation_time.timestamp():.0f}"
                
                order = {
                    "order_number": order_number,
                    "customer": {
                        "user_id": cart["user_id"],
                        # Would fetch user details here
                    },
                    "items": cart["items"],
                    "pricing": {
                        "subtotal": cart["subtotal"],
                        "discount": Decimal128("0.00"),
                        "tax": Decimal128("0.00"),      # calculated by tax service
                        "shipping": Decimal128("0.00"),
                        "total": cart["subtotal"]
                    },
                    "payment": payment_info,
                    "status": "created",
                    "status_history": [
                        {"status": "created", "at": datetime.now(), "by": "system"}
                    ],
                    "region": "US",
                    "created_at": datetime.now(),
                    "updated_at": datetime.now()
                }
                
                order_result = db.orders.insert_one(order, session=session)
                
                # ── Step 4: Mark cart as converted ─────────
                db.carts.update_one(
                    {"_id": cart_id},
                    {
                        "$set": {
                            "status": "converted",
                            "converted_to_order": order_result.inserted_id,
                            "updated_at": datetime.now()
                        }
                    },
                    session=session
                )
                
                # ── Step 5: Update customer stats ──────────
                db.users.update_one(
                    {"_id": cart["user_id"]},
                    {
                        "$inc": {
                            "stats.total_orders": 1,
                            "stats.total_spent": float(str(cart["subtotal"]))
                        },
                        "$set": {"stats.last_order_at": datetime.now()}
                    },
                    session=session
                )
                
                # ── COMMIT — all 5 operations atomic ──────
                print(f"Order {order_number} created successfully")
                return order_result.inserted_id
                
            except Exception as e:
                # ABORT — nothing happened
                print(f"Order failed: {e}")
                raise

# ── REDSHIFT EQUIVALENT (for reference) ──────────────────────
# BEGIN TRANSACTION;
#
# -- Check inventory
# SELECT qty FROM store.inventory
# WHERE product_id = 12345 AND warehouse_id = 'WH-US-EAST'
# FOR UPDATE;  -- row lock (Redshift doesn't support FOR UPDATE!)
#
# -- Decrement inventory
# UPDATE store.inventory SET qty = qty - 2
# WHERE product_id = 12345 AND warehouse_id = 'WH-US-EAST';
#
# -- Create order (across multiple tables)
# INSERT INTO store.orders (customer_id, total, status, ...)
# VALUES (1, 189.97, 'created', ...);
#
# INSERT INTO store.order_items (order_id, product_id, qty, price, ...)
# VALUES (LAST_INSERT_ID(), 12345, 2, 29.99, ...);
# -- ... repeat for each item
#
# -- Update cart
# UPDATE store.carts SET status = 'converted' WHERE cart_id = 'cart_001';
#
# -- Update customer stats
# UPDATE store.customers SET total_orders = total_orders + 1
# WHERE customer_id = 1;
#
# COMMIT;
#
# PROBLEMS:
# → 6+ tables modified in one transaction
# → No row-level locking in Redshift (table-level only!)
# → Concurrent orders would serialize → massive bottleneck
# → Latency: 500ms-2s per order → unacceptable at 50K orders/hour

📦 COMPONENT 4: PRODUCT SEARCH

Atlas Search Configuration (Reference: Project 29)

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// ATLAS SEARCH — Product Search Index
// Reference: Project 29 (Atlas Search deep dive)
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── Search Index Definition (created in Atlas UI or API) ──
{
    "name": "product_search",
    "mappings": {
        "dynamic": false,
        "fields": {
            "name": {
                "type": "string",
                "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
                "searchAnalyzer": "lucene.standard"
            },
            "brand": {
                "type": "string",
                "analyzer": "lucene.keyword"
            },
            "category": {
                "type": "string",
                "analyzer": "lucene.standard"
            },
            "search_keywords": {
                "type": "string",
                "analyzer": "lucene.standard"
            },
            "price.amount": {
                "type": "number"
            },
            "computed.avg_rating": {
                "type": "number"
            },
            "status": {
                "type": "string",
                "analyzer": "lucene.keyword"
            },
            "name_autocomplete": {
                "type": "autocomplete",
                "tokenization": "edgeGram",
                "minGrams": 2,
                "maxGrams": 15
            }
        }
    }
}

// ─── Search Query: "blue runnng shoes" (with typo + filters) ──
db.products.aggregate([
    {
        $search: {
            index: "product_search",
            compound: {
                must: [
                    {
                        text: {
                            query: "blue runnng shoes",     // typo: "runnng"
                            path: ["name", "search_keywords", "category"],
                            fuzzy: {
                                maxEdits: 2,                // allow 2 character edits
                                prefixLength: 3             // first 3 chars must match
                            }
                        }
                    }
                ],
                filter: [
                    {
                        equals: {
                            path: "status",
                            value: "active"
                        }
                    },
                    {
                        range: {
                            path: "price.amount",
                            gte: 50,
                            lte: 200
                        }
                    }
                ]
            },
            highlight: {
                path: ["name", "search_keywords"]
            }
        }
    },
    {
        $addFields: {
            search_score: { $meta: "searchScore" },
            highlights: { $meta: "searchHighlights" }
        }
    },
    // ── Faceted search (category counts, price ranges, ratings) ──
    {
        $facet: {
            results: [
                { $skip: 0 },
                { $limit: 20 },
                {
                    $project: {
                        name: 1,
                        brand: 1,
                        "price.amount": 1,
                        "price.compare_at": 1,
                        "computed.avg_rating": 1,
                        "computed.review_count": 1,
                        "images": { $arrayElemAt: ["$images", 0] },
                        search_score: 1,
                        highlights: 1
                    }
                }
            ],
            category_facets: [
                { $unwind: "$category" },
                { $group: { _id: "$category", count: { $sum: 1 } } },
                { $sort: { count: -1 } }
            ],
            price_ranges: [
                {
                    $bucket: {
                        groupBy: "$price.amount",
                        boundaries: [0, 25, 50, 100, 200, 500, 10000],
                        default: "500+",
                        output: { count: { $sum: 1 } }
                    }
                }
            ],
            rating_facets: [
                {
                    $bucket: {
                        groupBy: "$computed.avg_rating",
                        boundaries: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
                        output: { count: { $sum: 1 } }
                    }
                }
            ],
            total_count: [
                { $count: "total" }
            ]
        }
    }
])

// RESULT: <100ms response with:
// → Fuzzy-matched results (corrects "runnng" → "running")
// → Relevance-scored and sorted
// → Highlighted matching terms
// → Category facet counts
// → Price range facet counts
// → Rating distribution

// ── REDSHIFT EQUIVALENT (for reference) ──────────────────
// SELECT name, price_amount, avg_rating
// FROM store.products
// WHERE name ILIKE '%blue%'
//   AND name ILIKE '%running%'      -- no fuzzy matching
//   AND name ILIKE '%shoes%'
//   AND status = 'active'
//   AND price_amount BETWEEN 50 AND 200
// ORDER BY ... what? No relevance score available
// LIMIT 20;
//
// → Full table scan (ILIKE = no sort key usage)
// → No typo correction
// → No relevance scoring
// → No faceted counts in same query
// → No highlighting
// → Latency: 2-10 seconds on 2M products

📦 COMPONENT 5: KAFKA EVENT BACKBONE

MongoDB → Kafka → Redshift Pipeline (Reference: Project 44)

EVENT FLOW ARCHITECTURE:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     MONGODB CHANGE STREAMS                          │
│                                                                      │
│  products collection ──→ product.changes topic                       │
│  orders collection   ──→ order.events topic                          │
│  carts collection    ──→ cart.events topic                           │
│  users collection    ──→ user.events topic                           │
│  inventory changes   ──→ inventory.updates topic                     │
└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘
                            │
                            ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      APACHE KAFKA (MSK)                              │
│                                                                      │
│  Topics:                                                             │
│  ├── order.events          (50K msgs/hr peak)                        │
│  ├── product.changes       (5K msgs/hr)                              │
│  ├── cart.events           (200K msgs/hr peak)                       │
│  ├── user.events           (10K msgs/hr)                             │
│  ├── inventory.updates     (100K msgs/hr)                            │
│  ├── search.clickstream    (500K msgs/hr)                            │
│  └── payment.events        (50K msgs/hr)                             │
│                                                                      │
│  Consumer Groups:                                                    │
│  ├── redshift-sink         → Batch to Redshift every 5 min           │
│  ├── s3-archive            → Raw JSON to S3 data lake                │
│  ├── fraud-detector        → Real-time ML scoring                    │
│  ├── recommendation-engine → Update user preference model            │
│  ├── notification-service  → Email/push notifications                │
│  └── inventory-alerter     → Low stock warnings                      │
└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘
                            │
              ┌─────────────┼────────────────┐
              │             │                │
              ▼             ▼                ▼
    ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────────┐
    │  REDSHIFT     │  │  S3 DATA │   │  ML PIPELINE  │
    │              │  │  LAKE    │   │  (SageMaker)  │
    │ Analytical   │  │         │   │              │
    │ Tables:      │  │ Raw JSON│   │ Fraud model  │
    │ fact_orders  │  │ Parquet │   │ Recommend.   │
    │ dim_products │  │ Archive │   │ Forecasting  │
    │ dim_users    │  │         │   │              │
    │ fact_clicks  │  │         │   │              │
    └──────────────┘  └──────────┘   └──────────────┘

Kafka Connector Configuration

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MONGODB KAFKA SOURCE CONNECTOR — Order Events
# Reference: Project 44 (Kafka integration)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Connector configuration (deployed to Kafka Connect)
order_source_config = {
    "name": "mongodb-order-source",
    "config": {
        "connector.class": "com.mongodb.kafka.connect.MongoSourceConnector",
        "connection.uri": "mongodb+srv://kafka_user:pass@cluster0.mongodb.net/",
        "database": "ecommerce",
        "collection": "orders",
        
        # Only capture inserts and updates (not deletes)
        "publish.full.document.only": "true",
        "change.stream.full.document": "updateLookup",
        
        # Pipeline filter: only emit when status changes
        "pipeline": '[{"$match": {"operationType": {"$in": ["insert", "update"]}}}]',
        
        # Topic naming
        "topic.prefix": "novamart",
        # Results in topic: novamart.ecommerce.orders
        
        # Schema handling
        "output.format.value": "schema",
        "output.schema.infer.value": "true"
    }
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REDSHIFT SINK — Transform and Load Orders
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Custom Kafka consumer that transforms MongoDB documents
# into Redshift-compatible rows

import json
from kafka import KafkaConsumer
import psycopg2
from datetime import datetime

# Kafka consumer for order events
consumer = KafkaConsumer(
    'novamart.ecommerce.orders',
    bootstrap_servers=['kafka-broker-1:9092'],
    group_id='redshift-sink-orders',
    auto_offset_reset='earliest',
    value_deserializer=lambda m: json.loads(m.decode('utf-8'))
)

# Redshift connection
redshift = psycopg2.connect(
    host='novamart-cluster.xxxxx.redshift.amazonaws.com',
    port=5439,
    dbname='analytics',
    user='etl_user',
    password='secure_pass'
)

def transform_order_for_redshift(mongo_doc):
    """
    Transform MongoDB denormalized order document
    into Redshift normalized rows
    
    ONE MongoDB document becomes:
    → 1 row in fact_orders
    → N rows in fact_order_items (one per item)
    → Update to dim_customers (if new info)
    """
    
    # ── fact_orders row ──
    order_row = {
        'order_id': str(mongo_doc['_id']),
        'order_number': mongo_doc['order_number'],
        'customer_id': str(mongo_doc['customer']['user_id']),
        'status': mongo_doc['status'],
        'subtotal': float(str(mongo_doc['pricing']['subtotal'])),
        'discount': float(str(mongo_doc['pricing']['discount'])),
        'tax': float(str(mongo_doc['pricing']['tax'])),
        'shipping': float(str(mongo_doc['pricing']['shipping'])),
        'total': float(str(mongo_doc['pricing']['total'])),
        'payment_method': mongo_doc['payment']['method'],
        'shipping_city': mongo_doc['shipping']['address']['city'],
        'shipping_state': mongo_doc['shipping']['address']['state'],
        'shipping_country': mongo_doc['shipping']['address']['country'],
        'source': mongo_doc.get('source', 'unknown'),
        'region': mongo_doc.get('region', 'unknown'),
        'item_count': len(mongo_doc['items']),
        'created_at': mongo_doc['created_at'],
        'updated_at': mongo_doc['updated_at']
    }
    
    # ── fact_order_items rows ──
    item_rows = []
    for item in mongo_doc['items']:
        item_rows.append({
            'order_id': str(mongo_doc['_id']),
            'product_id': str(item['product_id']),
            'sku': item['sku'],
            'product_name': item['name'],
            'price': float(str(item['price'])),
            'quantity': item['quantity'],
            'subtotal': float(str(item['subtotal'])),
            'variant_size': item.get('variant', {}).get('size') if item.get('variant') else None,
            'variant_color': item.get('variant', {}).get('color') if item.get('variant') else None,
            'warehouse_id': item.get('warehouse_id')
        })
    
    return order_row, item_rows

# ── Batch processing: accumulate then bulk load ──
batch_orders = []
batch_items = []
BATCH_SIZE = 1000

for message in consumer:
    mongo_doc = message.value
    
    # Handle change stream format
    if 'fullDocument' in mongo_doc:
        mongo_doc = mongo_doc['fullDocument']
    
    order_row, item_rows = transform_order_for_redshift(mongo_doc)
    batch_orders.append(order_row)
    batch_items.extend(item_rows)
    
    if len(batch_orders) >= BATCH_SIZE:
        # Bulk load to Redshift
        load_to_redshift(batch_orders, batch_items)
        batch_orders = []
        batch_items = []

def load_to_redshift(orders, items):
    """
    Bulk load transformed data to Redshift
    Uses COPY from S3 for efficiency (not individual INSERTs)
    """
    import boto3
    import csv
    import io
    
    s3 = boto3.client('s3')
    cursor = redshift.cursor()
    
    # ── Write orders to S3 as CSV ──
    orders_csv = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.DictWriter(orders_csv, fieldnames=orders[0].keys())
    writer.writerows(orders)
    
    s3_key = f"etl/orders/{datetime.now().strftime('%Y/%m/%d/%H%M%S')}.csv"
    s3.put_object(
        Bucket='novamart-etl-staging',
        Key=s3_key,
        Body=orders_csv.getvalue()
    )
    
    # ── COPY into Redshift ──
    cursor.execute(f"""
        COPY analytics.fact_orders
        FROM 's3://novamart-etl-staging/{s3_key}'
        IAM_ROLE 'arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/redshift-etl'
        CSV
        TIMEFORMAT 'auto'
        TRUNCATECOLUMNS;
    """)
    
    # Same for items...
    
    redshift.commit()
    print(f"Loaded {len(orders)} orders, {len(items)} items to Redshift")


# ── REDSHIFT ANALYTICAL TABLES ────────────────────────────
#
# -- Star schema for analytics (normalized from MongoDB documents)
#
# CREATE TABLE analytics.fact_orders (
#     order_id        VARCHAR(24) PRIMARY KEY,
#     order_number    VARCHAR(50),
#     customer_id     VARCHAR

Redshift Star Schema — The Analytics Destination

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- REDSHIFT STAR SCHEMA — Analytics Data Warehouse
-- This is WHERE Redshift shines
-- MongoDB feeds this via Kafka pipeline (shown above)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- ── DIMENSION: Customers ─────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE analytics.dim_customers (
    customer_id         VARCHAR(24) PRIMARY KEY,     -- MongoDB ObjectId as string
    name                VARCHAR(255),
    email               VARCHAR(255),
    tier                VARCHAR(20),                  -- Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum
    signup_date         DATE,
    signup_source       VARCHAR(50),                  -- web/mobile/referral
    city                VARCHAR(100),
    state               VARCHAR(50),
    country             VARCHAR(5),
    region              VARCHAR(20),                  -- US/EU/ASIA
    first_order_date    DATE,
    last_order_date     DATE,
    lifetime_orders     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    lifetime_revenue    DECIMAL(12,2) DEFAULT 0,
    is_active           BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    updated_at          TIMESTAMP DEFAULT GETDATE()
)
DISTSTYLE KEY
DISTKEY(customer_id)                                  -- co-locate with fact_orders
SORTKEY(signup_date);                                 -- time-range queries

-- ── DIMENSION: Products ──────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE analytics.dim_products (
    product_id          VARCHAR(24) PRIMARY KEY,       -- MongoDB ObjectId
    sku                 VARCHAR(50),
    name                VARCHAR(255),
    brand               VARCHAR(100),
    category_l1         VARCHAR(100),                  -- Electronics
    category_l2         VARCHAR(100),                  -- Computer Accessories
    category_l3         VARCHAR(100),                  -- Mice
    price_current       DECIMAL(10,2),
    price_cost          DECIMAL(10,2),
    margin_pct          DECIMAL(5,2),                  -- computed
    status              VARCHAR(20),
    created_date        DATE,
    updated_at          TIMESTAMP DEFAULT GETDATE()
)
DISTSTYLE ALL                                          -- small dimension, replicate everywhere
SORTKEY(category_l1, brand);                           -- common analytical groupings

-- ── DIMENSION: Date ──────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE analytics.dim_date (
    date_key            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,            -- 20240531
    full_date           DATE,
    day_of_week         SMALLINT,
    day_name            VARCHAR(10),
    day_of_month        SMALLINT,
    day_of_year         SMALLINT,
    week_of_year        SMALLINT,
    month_number        SMALLINT,
    month_name          VARCHAR(10),
    quarter             SMALLINT,
    year                SMALLINT,
    is_weekend          BOOLEAN,
    is_holiday          BOOLEAN,
    fiscal_quarter      SMALLINT,
    fiscal_year         SMALLINT
)
DISTSTYLE ALL                                          -- tiny table, replicate
SORTKEY(full_date);

-- ── DIMENSION: Warehouses ────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE analytics.dim_warehouses (
    warehouse_id        VARCHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY,
    warehouse_name      VARCHAR(100),
    city                VARCHAR(100),
    state               VARCHAR(50),
    country             VARCHAR(5),
    region              VARCHAR(20),
    capacity_units      INTEGER,
    opened_date         DATE
)
DISTSTYLE ALL
SORTKEY(region);

-- ── FACT: Orders ─────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE analytics.fact_orders (
    order_id            VARCHAR(24) NOT NULL,           -- MongoDB ObjectId
    order_number        VARCHAR(50),
    customer_id         VARCHAR(24),                    -- FK to dim_customers
    date_key            INTEGER,                        -- FK to dim_date
    status              VARCHAR(20),
    subtotal            DECIMAL(12,2),
    discount            DECIMAL(12,2),
    tax                 DECIMAL(12,2),
    shipping_cost       DECIMAL(12,2),
    total               DECIMAL(12,2),
    payment_method      VARCHAR(30),
    shipping_city       VARCHAR(100),
    shipping_state      VARCHAR(50),
    shipping_country    VARCHAR(5),
    source              VARCHAR(20),                    -- web/mobile/api
    region              VARCHAR(20),
    item_count          SMALLINT,
    coupon_applied      BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    created_at          TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at          TIMESTAMP
)
DISTSTYLE KEY
DISTKEY(customer_id)                                    -- co-locate with dim_customers
COMPOUND SORTKEY(created_at, status);                   -- time + status filtering

-- ── FACT: Order Items (grain = one item per row) ─────
CREATE TABLE analytics.fact_order_items (
    order_id            VARCHAR(24) NOT NULL,
    product_id          VARCHAR(24),                    -- FK to dim_products
    customer_id         VARCHAR(24),                    -- denormalized for direct joins
    date_key            INTEGER,                        -- denormalized
    sku                 VARCHAR(50),
    product_name        VARCHAR(255),
    unit_price          DECIMAL(10,2),
    quantity            SMALLINT,
    line_total          DECIMAL(12,2),
    variant_size        VARCHAR(20),
    variant_color       VARCHAR(50),
    warehouse_id        VARCHAR(20),                    -- FK to dim_warehouses
    created_at          TIMESTAMP
)
DISTSTYLE KEY
DISTKEY(customer_id)
COMPOUND SORTKEY(created_at, product_id);

-- ── FACT: Clickstream (from Kafka → S3 → Spectrum) ──
-- Too large for Redshift local storage → use Spectrum
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE analytics_spectrum.fact_clickstream (
    event_id            VARCHAR(50),
    user_id             VARCHAR(24),
    session_id          VARCHAR(50),
    event_type          VARCHAR(30),                    -- page_view/click/search/add_to_cart
    page_url            VARCHAR(1000),
    product_id          VARCHAR(24),
    search_query        VARCHAR(500),
    referrer            VARCHAR(1000),
    device_type         VARCHAR(20),
    browser             VARCHAR(50),
    country             VARCHAR(5),
    event_timestamp     TIMESTAMP
)
STORED AS PARQUET
LOCATION 's3://novamart-data-lake/clickstream/'
TABLE PROPERTIES ('parquet.compress'='SNAPPY');

-- ── MATERIALIZED VIEWS (pre-aggregated analytics) ────
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.mv_daily_revenue AS
SELECT
    d.full_date,
    d.day_name,
    d.month_name,
    d.quarter,
    d.year,
    p.category_l1,
    p.brand,
    w.region AS warehouse_region,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fo.order_id)     AS order_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fo.customer_id)  AS unique_customers,
    SUM(foi.quantity)               AS units_sold,
    SUM(foi.line_total)             AS gross_revenue,
    SUM(fo.discount)                AS total_discounts,
    SUM(fo.total)                   AS net_revenue,
    AVG(fo.total)                   AS avg_order_value
FROM analytics.fact_order_items foi
JOIN analytics.fact_orders fo       ON foi.order_id = fo.order_id
JOIN analytics.dim_date d           ON fo.date_key = d.date_key
JOIN analytics.dim_products p       ON foi.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN analytics.dim_warehouses w     ON foi.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8;

-- Refresh nightly after ETL completes
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.mv_daily_revenue;

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW analytics.mv_customer_cohorts AS
SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', c.signup_date)  AS cohort_month,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', fo.created_at)  AS activity_month,
    DATEDIFF('month', c.signup_date, fo.created_at) AS months_since_signup,
    COUNT(DISTINCT fo.customer_id)      AS active_customers,
    SUM(fo.total)                       AS cohort_revenue,
    AVG(fo.total)                       AS avg_order_value
FROM analytics.fact_orders fo
JOIN analytics.dim_customers c ON fo.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;

Redshift Analytical Queries — What Redshift Does Best

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- ANALYTICAL QUERIES — Redshift's Strength
-- These queries are IMPOSSIBLE or IMPRACTICAL in MongoDB
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- ── QUERY 1: Revenue Trend with Year-over-Year Comparison ──
SELECT
    d.year,
    d.quarter,
    d.month_name,
    SUM(fo.total)                                   AS monthly_revenue,
    LAG(SUM(fo.total), 12) OVER (ORDER BY d.year, d.month_number)
                                                     AS same_month_last_year,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(fo.total) - LAG(SUM(fo.total), 12) OVER (ORDER BY d.year, d.month_number))
        / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(fo.total), 12) OVER (ORDER BY d.year, d.month_number), 0)
        * 100, 2
    )                                                AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM analytics.fact_orders fo
JOIN analytics.dim_date d ON fo.date_key = d.date_key
WHERE fo.status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'refunded')
GROUP BY d.year, d.quarter, d.month_name, d.month_number
ORDER BY d.year, d.month_number;

-- WHY REDSHIFT WINS: scans billions of rows across years
--   columnar storage reads ONLY total and date columns
--   window function LAG computed across sorted partitions
--   MongoDB: would require $setWindowFields on massive dataset → very slow

-- ── QUERY 2: Customer Cohort Retention Analysis ──────
WITH cohort_base AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', MIN(created_at)) AS cohort_month
    FROM analytics.fact_orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
monthly_activity AS (
    SELECT
        cb.cohort_month,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', fo.created_at)              AS activity_month,
        DATEDIFF('month', cb.cohort_month,
                 DATE_TRUNC('month', fo.created_at))    AS month_number,
        COUNT(DISTINCT fo.customer_id)                   AS active_users
    FROM analytics.fact_orders fo
    JOIN cohort_base cb ON fo.customer_id = cb.customer_id
    GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
),
cohort_size AS (
    SELECT cohort_month, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS cohort_size
    FROM cohort_base
    GROUP BY cohort_month
)
SELECT
    ma.cohort_month,
    cs.cohort_size,
    ma.month_number,
    ma.active_users,
    ROUND(ma.active_users::FLOAT / cs.cohort_size * 100, 2) AS retention_pct
FROM monthly_activity ma
JOIN cohort_size cs ON ma.cohort_month = cs.cohort_month
WHERE ma.month_number BETWEEN 0 AND 12
ORDER BY ma.cohort_month, ma.month_number;

-- WHY REDSHIFT WINS: multiple CTEs, window-style analysis
--   across millions of customers over years of data
--   distribution by customer_id → cohort JOINs are local
--   MongoDB: $lookup across millions → out of memory or hours-long

-- ── QUERY 3: Product Demand Forecasting Input ────────
SELECT
    p.category_l1,
    p.category_l2,
    p.brand,
    d.year,
    d.week_of_year,
    d.is_holiday,
    SUM(foi.quantity)                                     AS units_sold,
    SUM(foi.line_total)                                   AS revenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT foi.order_id)                           AS order_count,
    AVG(foi.unit_price)                                    AS avg_selling_price,
    STDDEV(foi.quantity)                                    AS demand_volatility,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY foi.quantity) AS median_qty,
    -- Rolling 4-week average
    AVG(SUM(foi.quantity)) OVER (
        PARTITION BY p.category_l1, p.brand
        ORDER BY d.year, d.week_of_year
        ROWS BETWEEN 3 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    )                                                      AS rolling_4wk_avg
FROM analytics.fact_order_items foi
JOIN analytics.dim_products p ON foi.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN analytics.dim_date d ON foi.date_key = d.date_key
WHERE d.year >= 2022
GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5,6
ORDER BY 1,2,3,4,5;

-- WHY REDSHIFT WINS: STDDEV, PERCENTILE_CONT, rolling windows
--   across 3 years of weekly data across all categories
--   This feeds directly into SageMaker forecasting model
--   MongoDB: no PERCENTILE_CONT, limited window functions on large data

-- ── QUERY 4: Cross-sell / Market Basket Analysis ─────
WITH item_pairs AS (
    SELECT
        a.product_id AS product_a,
        b.product_id AS product_b,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.order_id) AS co_purchase_count
    FROM analytics.fact_order_items a
    JOIN analytics.fact_order_items b
        ON a.order_id = b.order_id
        AND a.product_id < b.product_id      -- avoid duplicates
    GROUP BY 1, 2
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.order_id) >= 10   -- minimum support
),
product_counts AS (
    SELECT product_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS total_orders
    FROM analytics.fact_order_items
    GROUP BY product_id
)
SELECT
    pa.name AS product_a_name,
    pb.name AS product_b_name,
    ip.co_purchase_count,
    pca.total_orders AS product_a_orders,
    pcb.total_orders AS product_b_orders,
    ROUND(ip.co_purchase_count::FLOAT / pca.total_orders * 100, 2)
        AS confidence_a_to_b,
    ROUND(ip.co_purchase_count::FLOAT / pcb.total_orders * 100, 2)
        AS confidence_b_to_a
FROM item_pairs ip
JOIN analytics.dim_products pa ON ip.product_a = pa.product_id
JOIN analytics.dim_products pb ON ip.product_b = pb.product_id
JOIN product_counts pca ON ip.product_a = pca.product_id
JOIN product_counts pcb ON ip.product_b = pcb.product_id
ORDER BY ip.co_purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 100;

-- WHY REDSHIFT WINS: self-join on fact table (millions × millions)
--   distribution by customer_id helps with co-located joins
--   MPP parallelism across nodes handles the combinatorial explosion
--   MongoDB: self-$lookup on large collections → impractical

📦 COMPONENT 6: USER PROFILES & PERSONALIZATION

MongoDB Document Design (References: Projects 6, 19, 20)

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// USER PROFILE — MongoDB Document Model
// Serves: real-time API, personalization, authentication
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

{
    _id: ObjectId("user_001"),
    
    // ── Authentication ──
    auth: {
        email: "sarah.johnson@example.com",
        password_hash: "$2b$12$...",                  // bcrypt
        mfa_enabled: true,
        mfa_method: "totp",
        last_login: ISODate("2024-05-31T09:00:00Z"),
        login_history: [                               // Subset: last 5 only
            { at: ISODate("2024-05-31T09:00:00Z"), ip: "203.0.113.42", device: "Chrome/Mac" },
            { at: ISODate("2024-05-30T14:22:00Z"), ip: "203.0.113.42", device: "iOS App" }
            // ... last 5
        ]
    },
    
    // ── Profile ──
    profile: {
        first_name: "Sarah",
        last_name: "Johnson",
        display_name: "Sarah J.",
        phone: "+1-512-555-0123",
        date_of_birth: ISODate("1990-03-15"),
        gender: "female",
        avatar_url: "https://cdn.novamart.com/avatars/user_001.jpg",
        locale: "en-US",
        timezone: "America/Chicago"
    },
    
    // ── Addresses (multiple per user) ──
    addresses: [
        {
            _id: ObjectId("addr_001"),
            label: "Home",
            street: "123 Oak Lane",
            city: "Austin",
            state: "TX",
            zip: "78701",
            country: "US",
            is_default_shipping: true,
            is_default_billing: true
        },
        {
            _id: ObjectId("addr_002"),
            label: "Office",
            street: "456 Tech Blvd, Suite 200",
            city: "Austin",
            state: "TX",
            zip: "78702",
            country: "US",
            is_default_shipping: false,
            is_default_billing: false
        }
    ],
    
    // ── Payment Methods (encrypted — Project 38 CSFLE) ──
    payment_methods: [
        {
            _id: ObjectId("pm_001"),
            type: "credit_card",
            brand: "Visa",
            last_four: "[REDACTED:CREDIT_DEBIT_NUMBER]",                        // only last 4 stored
            exp_month: 12,
            exp_year: 2026,
            billing_address_id: ObjectId("addr_001"),
            is_default: true,
            stripe_token: "tok_encrypted_xxx"          // CSFLE encrypted
        }
    ],
    
    // ── Membership & Loyalty ──
    membership: {
        tier: "Gold",
        points: 15820,
        tier_qualified_at: ISODate("2024-01-15"),
        tier_expires_at: ISODate("2025-01-15"),
        tier_history: [
            { tier: "Bronze", from: ISODate("2023-01-01"), to: ISODate("2023-06-15") },
            { tier: "Silver", from: ISODate("2023-06-15"), to: ISODate("2024-01-15") },
            { tier: "Gold",   from: ISODate("2024-01-15"), to: null }
        ]
    },
    
    // ── Computed Stats (Project 20 — updated via $inc) ──
    stats: {
        total_orders: 47,
        total_spent: 4823.50,
        avg_order_value: 102.63,
        last_order_at: ISODate("2024-05-28T16:30:00Z"),
        first_order_at: ISODate("2023-01-15T10:00:00Z"),
        favorite_categories: ["Electronics", "Books", "Footwear"],
        favorite_brands: ["TechCo", "AthletePro"]
    },
    
    // ── Personalization Preferences ──
    preferences: {
        notifications: {
            email_marketing: true,
            push_order_updates: true,
            push_promotions: false,
            sms_enabled: false
        },
        display: {
            currency: "USD",
            language: "en",
            items_per_page: 24
        },
        privacy: {
            share_activity: false,
            personalized_ads: true
        }
    },
    
    // ── Recommendations (precomputed by ML pipeline) ──
    // Subset pattern: top 20 recommendations, full model in ML service
    recommendations: {
        updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T03:00:00Z"),
        products: [
            { product_id: ObjectId("prod_xxx"), score: 0.95, reason: "based_on_purchase" },
            { product_id: ObjectId("prod_yyy"), score: 0.89, reason: "similar_users" },
            // ... top 20
        ]
    },
    
    // ── GDPR / Data Governance ──
    data_governance: {
        consent_marketing: { granted: true, at: ISODate("2023-01-15") },
        consent_analytics: { granted: true, at: ISODate("2023-01-15") },
        data_region: "US",                            // for zone sharding (Project 39)
        deletion_requested: false,
        export_requested: false
    },
    
    // ── Metadata ──
    created_at: ISODate("2023-01-15T10:00:00Z"),
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:20:00Z"),
    version: 34
}

📦 COMPONENT 7: INVENTORY MANAGEMENT

Real-Time Inventory — MongoDB (References: Projects 20, 25, 30)

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// INVENTORY — Real-Time Stock Management
// Pattern: Computed (Project 20) + Change Streams (Project 25)
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── Inventory Document (per product per warehouse) ──
{
    _id: ObjectId("inv_001"),
    product_id: ObjectId("prod_electronics_001"),
    sku: "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
    warehouse_id: "WH-US-EAST",
    
    // ── Current Stock ──
    quantity: {
        on_hand: 450,                // physically in warehouse
        reserved: 12,                // in carts / pending orders
        available: 438,              // on_hand - reserved (computed)
        incoming: 200,               // purchase orders in transit
        damaged: 3                   // quarantined
    },
    
    // ── Thresholds ──
    thresholds: {
        reorder_point: 100,          // trigger PO when available < 100
        reorder_quantity: 500,
        safety_stock: 50,
        max_stock: 1000
    },
    
    // ── Location within warehouse ──
    location: {
        zone: "A",
        aisle: "12",
        shelf: "C",
        bin: "4"
    },
    
    // ── Audit trail (last 10 movements — Subset pattern) ──
    recent_movements: [
        {
            type: "sale",
            quantity: -2,
            order_id: ObjectId("order_001"),
            at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:00Z"),
            by: "order_service"
        },
        {
            type: "receipt",
            quantity: 200,
            po_number: "PO-2024-1234",
            at: ISODate("2024-05-30T08:00:00Z"),
            by: "receiving_dock"
        }
        // ... last 10
    ],
    
    updated_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:25:00Z")
}

// ─── INDEXES ─────────────────────────────────────────
db.inventory.createIndex({ product_id: 1, warehouse_id: 1 }, { unique: true })
db.inventory.createIndex({ "quantity.available": 1 })      // find low stock
db.inventory.createIndex({ sku: 1 })

// ─── ATOMIC STOCK RESERVATION (during add-to-cart) ───
// Decrement available, increment reserved — ONE atomic operation
db.inventory.updateOne(
    {
        product_id: ObjectId("prod_electronics_001"),
        warehouse_id: "WH-US-EAST",
        "quantity.available": { $gte: 2 }                 // check stock first
    },
    {
        $inc: {
            "quantity.reserved": 2,
            "quantity.available": -2
        },
        $push: {
            recent_movements: {
                $each: [{
                    type: "reservation",
                    quantity: -2,
                    cart_id: ObjectId("cart_001"),
                    at: new Date(),
                    by: "cart_service"
                }],
                $slice: -10                                // keep only last 10
            }
        },
        $set: { updated_at: new Date() }
    }
)
// If quantity.available < 2 → update matches 0 documents → no change → "out of stock"

// ─── CHANGE STREAM: Low Stock Alert ─────────────────
// Reference: Project 25
const pipeline = [
    {
        $match: {
            "updateDescription.updatedFields.quantity.available": { $exists: true }
        }
    }
]

const changeStream = db.inventory.watch(pipeline)
changeStream.on("change", (change) => {
    const available = change.updateDescription.updatedFields["quantity.available"]
    if (available !== undefined) {
        // Check against threshold (would fetch document for threshold)
        if (available < 100) {
            console.log(`LOW STOCK ALERT: ${change.documentKey._id} → ${available} units`)
            // Trigger reorder workflow
        }
    }
})

📦 COMPONENT 8: NOTIFICATION SERVICE

MongoDB Time Series for Notification Delivery Tracking (Reference: Project 30)

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// NOTIFICATIONS — MongoDB Document + Time Series
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── Notification Queue Collection ───────────────────
{
    _id: ObjectId("notif_001"),
    user_id: ObjectId("user_001"),
    type: "order_shipped",                   // order_shipped | promo | low_stock | review_request
    channel: "email",                        // email | push | sms
    
    // ── Content ──
    content: {
        subject: "Your order NM-2024-0531-78234 has shipped!",
        body: "Hi Sarah, your order is on its way...",
        template_id: "tmpl_order_shipped_v3",
        variables: {
            order_number: "NM-2024-0531-78234",
            tracking_url: "https://track.novamart.com/...",
            estimated_delivery: "June 5, 2024"
        }
    },
    
    // ── Delivery status ──
    status: "delivered",
    attempts: [
        { at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:30:00Z"), result: "delivered", provider: "sendgrid" }
    ],
    
    // ── Scheduling ──
    scheduled_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:30:00Z"),
    sent_at: ISODate("2024-05-31T10:30:01Z"),
    
    // ── TTL: auto-delete after 90 days ──
    expires_at: ISODate("2024-08-29T10:30:00Z")
}

// TTL index for auto-cleanup
db.notifications.createIndex({ expires_at: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 0 })

// Compound index for user notification history
db.notifications.createIndex({ user_id: 1, sent_at: -1 })

// Index for processing queue
db.notifications.createIndex({ status: 1, scheduled_at: 1 })

📦 COMPLETE INDEXING STRATEGY — All Collections

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// COMPREHENSIVE INDEX STRATEGY
// References: Projects 9-11 (indexing deep dive)
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── PRODUCTS ────────────────────────────────────────
db.products.createIndex({ sku: 1 }, { unique: true })
db.products.createIndex({ slug: 1 }, { unique: true })
db.products.createIndex({ category: 1, "computed.avg_rating": -1 })
db.products.createIndex({ brand: 1, status: 1 })
db.products.createIndex({ "price.amount": 1 })
db.products.createIndex({ status: 1, created_at: -1 })
db.products.createIndex(
    { "inventory.total_available": 1 },
    { partialFilterExpression: { status: "active" } }       // Partial index (Project 11)
)
// Atlas Search index handles text search (defined separately)

// ─── CARTS ───────────────────────────────────────────
db.carts.createIndex({ user_id: 1 }, { sparse: true })
db.carts.createIndex({ session_id: 1 }, { sparse: true })
db.carts.createIndex({ expires_at: 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 0 })   // TTL
db.carts.createIndex({ status: 1, updated_at: -1 })

// ─── ORDERS ──────────────────────────────────────────
db.orders.createIndex({ order_number: 1 }, { unique: true })
db.orders.createIndex({ "customer.user_id": 1, created_at: -1 })     // order history
db.orders.createIndex({ status: 1, created_at: -1 })
db.orders.createIndex({ "items.product_id": 1 })                     // product order lookup
db.orders.createIndex({ region: 1, created_at: -1 })                 // zone sharding prep

// ─── USERS ───────────────────────────────────────────
db.users.createIndex({ "auth.email": 1 }, { unique: true })
db.users.createIndex({ "membership.tier": 1 })
db.users.createIndex({ "data_governance.data_region": 1 })            // zone sharding
db.users.createIndex({ "stats.last_order_at": -1 })

// ─── INVENTORY ───────────────────────────────────────
db.inventory.createIndex({ product_id: 1, warehouse_id: 1 }, { unique: true })
db.inventory.createIndex({ sku: 1, warehouse_id: 1 })
db.inventory.createIndex(
    { "quantity.available": 1 },
    { partialFilterExpression: { "quantity.available": { $lt: 100 } } }
)

// ─── REVIEWS (separate collection — referenced from products) ───
db.reviews.createIndex({ product_id: 1, created_at: -1 })
db.reviews.createIndex({ user_id: 1, created_at: -1 })
db.reviews.createIndex({ product_id: 1, rating: -1 })

📦 SHARDING STRATEGY — Global Distribution

Zone Sharding for GDPR Compliance (References: Projects 21-22, 39, 41)

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SHARDING CONFIGURATION — Global Cluster
// References: Project 21 (sharding), 22 (shard keys),
//             39 (zone sharding), 41 (global clusters)
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// ─── SHARD KEY DECISIONS ─────────────────────────────

// Products: hashed on _id (even distribution, any product from any region)
