sh.shardCollection("ecommerce.products", { _id: "hashed" })

// Orders: compound key { region: 1, customer.user_id: 1 }
// → Zone sharding by region (GDPR) + targeted queries by customer
sh.shardCollection("ecommerce.orders", { "region": 1, "customer.user_id": 1 })

// Users: compound key { data_governance.data_region: 1, _id: 1 }
// → EU users stay in EU shards (GDPR Article 44)
sh.shardCollection("ecommerce.users", { "data_governance.data_region": 1, "_id": 1 })

// Carts: hashed on user_id (even distribution, fast user lookup)
sh.shardCollection("ecommerce.carts", { "user_id": "hashed" })

// Inventory: by warehouse_id (co-locate warehouse inventory)
sh.shardCollection("ecommerce.inventory", { "warehouse_id": 1, "product_id": 1 })

// ─── ZONE SHARDING FOR GDPR ─────────────────────────

// Add zone tags to shards
sh.addShardTag("shard-us-east-1", "US")
sh.addShardTag("shard-us-west-1", "US")
sh.addShardTag("shard-eu-central-1", "EU")
sh.addShardTag("shard-eu-west-1", "EU")
sh.addShardTag("shard-ap-east-1", "ASIA")

// Assign key ranges to zones
// Users collection: EU users on EU shards
sh.addTagRange(
    "ecommerce.users",
    { "data_governance.data_region": "EU", "_id": MinKey },
    { "data_governance.data_region": "EU", "_id": MaxKey },
    "EU"
)
sh.addTagRange(
    "ecommerce.users",
    { "data_governance.data_region": "US", "_id": MinKey },
    { "data_governance.data_region": "US", "_id": MaxKey },
    "US"
)
sh.addTagRange(
    "ecommerce.users",
    { "data_governance.data_region": "ASIA", "_id": MinKey },
    { "data_governance.data_region": "ASIA", "_id": MaxKey },
    "ASIA"
)

// Orders collection: same zone pattern
sh.addTagRange(
    "ecommerce.orders",
    { "region": "EU", "customer.user_id": MinKey },
    { "region": "EU", "customer.user_id": MaxKey },
    "EU"
)
sh.addTagRange(
    "ecommerce.orders",
    { "region": "US", "customer.user_id": MinKey },
    { "region": "US", "customer.user_id": MaxKey },
    "US"
)

// RESULT:
// → EU user data NEVER leaves EU shards → GDPR compliant
// → US user queries only hit US shards → low latency
// → Redshift equivalent: separate clusters per region
//   (no native zone-based data locality within one cluster)

📊 SIDE-BY-SIDE COMPARISON TABLE — Full Architecture

┌────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┐
│ ASPECT                  │ MONGODB (Operational)       │ REDSHIFT (Analytical)          │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Primary Purpose         │ Serve the application       │ Serve the analysts             │
│                         │ Real-time read/write        │ Complex analytical queries     │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Latency Target          │ <50ms per operation         │ <30s per analytical query      │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Concurrency             │ 100K+ ops/second            │ ~50-500 concurrent queries     │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Data Model              │ Denormalized documents      │ Normalized star schema         │
│                         │ 7 collections               │ 4 dimension + 3 fact tables    │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Schema                  │ Flexible (polymorphic)      │ Fixed (DDL-enforced)           │
│                         │ Products vary by type       │ Every row same columns         │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Data Freshness          │ Real-time (current state)   │ Near-real-time (5-min lag)     │
│                         │ Milliseconds old            │ ETL pipeline delay             │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Search Capability       │ Atlas Search (Lucene)       │ LIKE/ILIKE (limited)           │
│                         │ Fuzzy, facets, autocomplete │ No relevance scoring           │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Scaling Model           │ Horizontal sharding         │ Vertical + node addition       │
│                         │ Add shards for more         │ Resize or add compute nodes    │
│                         │ throughput                  │ for more scan capacity         │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Geographic              │ Zone sharding (data stays   │ Separate clusters per region   │
│ Distribution            │ in region natively)         │ Data sharing across regions    │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Event Processing        │ Change streams (native)     │ No native event emission       │
│                         │ Real-time CDC               │ Must poll or use external CDC  │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Window Functions        │ $setWindowFields (limited)  │ Full SQL window functions      │
│                         │ Good for small-medium sets  │ Optimized for massive scans    │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ JOINs / Cross-entity    │ $lookup (expensive)         │ Distributed JOINs (optimized)  │
│ Analysis                │ Prefer embedding instead    │ Co-located via DISTKEY         │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Historical Analysis     │ Not ideal (document scans)  │ Designed for this (columnar)   │
│ (years of data)         │ Aggregation pipeline slow   │ Zone maps + compression        │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Transactions            │ Multi-doc ACID (with cost)  │ Full SQL transactions          │
│                         │ Design to minimize need     │ Always transactional           │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Data Volume (this arch) │ ~500GB active operational   │ ~5TB historical analytical     │
│                         │ Hot data, frequently updated│ Cold/warm, append-mostly       │
├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤
│ Estimated Cost/Month    │ ~$3,000-5,000 (Atlas M40)  │ ~$5,000-8,000 (RA3 4-node)    │
│ (this architecture)     │ Global cluster adds more    │ + Spectrum for clickstream     │
└────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┘

✅ WHAT SHOULD DO (Architecture Best Practices)

SHOULD DO:

✅ CLEAR OWNERSHIP BOUNDARIES
    → MongoDB OWNS: current state of all operational entities
       (products, carts, orders, users, inventory)
    → Redshift OWNS: historical analytics and cross-entity analysis
       (revenue trends, cohort retention, demand forecasting)
    → Kafka OWNS: event transport between the two
    → S3 OWNS: raw event archive and data lake
    → NEVER have two systems as "source of truth" for same data

✅ SINGLE DIRECTION DATA FLOW (for most data)
    → MongoDB → Kafka → Redshift (primary flow)
    → Redshift → MongoDB (only for ML model outputs / precomputed insights)
    → AVOID bidirectional sync (complexity nightmare)

✅ EVENTUAL CONSISTENCY BETWEEN SYSTEMS
    → Accept 1-5 minute lag between MongoDB and Redshift
    → Redshift reports say "as of 10:25 AM" not "real-time"
    → If analyst needs real-time: use MongoDB Charts (Project 42)

✅ IDEMPOTENT ETL PIPELINE
    → Kafka consumers must handle duplicate messages
    → Redshift UPSERT pattern: DELETE + INSERT (or MERGE in newer versions)
    → Use MongoDB change stream resume tokens for exactly-once semantics

✅ SCHEMA EVOLUTION STRATEGY
    → MongoDB: add fields freely, application handles missing fields
    → Redshift: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN when new fields appear in ETL
    → ETL layer handles the transformation / schema mapping

✅ MONITORING BOTH SYSTEMS
    → MongoDB Atlas: real-time metrics, slow query analyzer
    → Redshift: CloudWatch + STL/SVL system tables
    → Kafka: consumer lag monitoring (critical for data freshness)
    → Unified dashboard: Grafana or Datadog aggregating all three

❌ WHAT SHOULD NOT DO (Anti-Patterns)

DO NOT:

❌ USE REDSHIFT FOR PRODUCT PAGE API
    → "But we already have Redshift, why add MongoDB?"
    → Because 50K concurrent product page requests → Redshift WLM dies
    → Redshift minimum latency 100ms+ → user experience degrades
    → This is the #1 mistake Redshift teams make

❌ USE MONGODB FOR 3-YEAR REVENUE TREND ANALYSIS
    → "But MongoDB has aggregation pipeline"
    → Scanning 500M orders with $group across 3 years → hours
    → Redshift scans ONLY the revenue column (columnar) → seconds
    → This is the #1 mistake MongoDB teams make

❌ DUPLICATE BUSINESS LOGIC ACROSS BOTH SYSTEMS
    → "Let's compute revenue in MongoDB AND Redshift"
    → Numbers WILL diverge → trust is destroyed
    → Rule: operational metrics in MongoDB, analytical metrics in Redshift
    → Clear ownership prevents conflicting numbers

❌ SKIP THE KAFKA LAYER
    → "Let's just read from MongoDB replica and write to Redshift directly"
    → Tight coupling → if Redshift is down, pipeline breaks
    → No replay capability → data loss on failure
    → No fan-out → can't add ML pipeline later
    → Kafka decouples producers from consumers (Project 44)

❌ SHARD MONGODB BY DATE ALONE
    → Tempting for Redshift users (sort key = date)
    → Date-based shard key → hot shard (all writes go to latest date)
    → Use compound shard key: { region: 1, customer_id: 1 }
    → Reference: Project 22 (shard key selection)

❌ NORMALIZE MONGODB LIKE REDSHIFT
    → "Let me create orders, order_items, order_payments as separate collections"
    → Now you need $lookup (JOIN) for every order read
    → MongoDB $lookup is 10-100x slower than Redshift distributed JOIN
    → EMBED items and payment inside order document
    → Reference: Project 14 (embedding vs referencing)

❌ IGNORE SECURITY BOUNDARIES
    → App service should NOT have access to Redshift
    → Analytics users should NOT have write access to MongoDB
    → Kafka service account: read from MongoDB, write to topics
    → ETL service account: read from Kafka, write to Redshift
    → Reference: Project 37 (RBAC)

⚙️ HOW IT ONLY WORKS INTERNALLY — Full Data Flow

COMPLETE DATA FLOW: User Buys a Product

TIME 0ms — User clicks "Place Order" on website
    │
    ▼
TIME 1ms — API Gateway routes to Order Service
    │
    ▼
TIME 2-15ms — MongoDB multi-document transaction (Project 28)
    │
    ├── Read cart document
    │   └── WiredTiger: B-tree lookup on _id → cache hit → 1ms
    │
    ├── Verify + decrement inventory (per item)
    │   └── WiredTiger: compound index lookup → atomic $inc → 2ms per item
    │
    ├── Create order document (full snapshot of items, prices, addresses)
    │   └── WiredTiger: BSON serialize → write to journal → write to collection → 3ms
    │   └── Oplog: insert operation recorded → change stream fires
    │
    ├── Mark cart as converted
    │   └── WiredTiger: update status field → 1ms
    │
    └── Update user stats ($inc total_orders, total_spent)
        └── WiredTiger: atomic field update → 1ms
    
    TOTAL MONGODB TIME: ~15ms
    All operations on PRIMARY node
    Replicated to SECONDARIES via oplog asynchronously

TIME 15ms — Transaction committed → response sent to user
    │
    ▼
TIME 16ms — MongoDB oplog entry propagated
    │
    ├── Replica set secondaries receive oplog entry
    │   └── Secondary 1 (same region): ~5ms replication lag
    │   └── Secondary 2 (cross-region): ~50-100ms replication lag
    │
    └── Change stream fires (Project 25)
        └── Kafka source connector captures the event
    │
    ▼
TIME 50ms — Kafka receives order.created event
    │
    ├── Topic: novamart.ecommerce.orders
    │   Partition key: region + customer_id
    │   Message: full order document as JSON
    │
    ├── Consumer: notification-service
    │   └── Reads event → sends "Order Confirmed" email via SendGrid
    │   └── Writes notification document to MongoDB notifications collection
    │
    ├── Consumer: fraud-detector
    │   └── Reads event → runs ML model (SageMaker endpoint)
    │   └── Score > 0.8 → flag order → update MongoDB order status
    │   └── Latency: ~100ms for ML inference
    │
    ├── Consumer: recommendation-engine
    │   └── Reads event → updates user preference model
    │   └── Writes updated recommendations back to MongoDB user document
    │
    └── Consumer: redshift-sink (batched)
        └── Accumulates events in memory buffer
        └── Every 5 minutes OR 1000 events (whichever first):
            │
            ▼
TIME 5 min — Redshift ETL batch fires
    │
    ├── Transform: MongoDB document → Redshift rows
    │   └── One order document → 1 fact_orders row + N fact_order_items rows
    │   └── Denormalized document → normalized star schema
    │   └── Decimal128 → DECIMAL(12,2)
    │   └── ObjectId → VARCHAR(24)
    │   └── ISODate → TIMESTAMP
    │
    ├── Stage: Write CSV/Parquet to S3 staging bucket
    │   └── s3://novamart-etl-staging/orders/2024/05/31/103000.parquet
    │
    ├── Load: COPY into Redshift
    │   └── COPY analytics.fact_orders FROM 's3://...' IAM_ROLE '...' PARQUET;
    │   └── Redshift leader node distributes rows to compute slices
    │   └── Distribution by customer_id → row goes to correct slice
    │   └── Sort key (created_at) → row sorted within block
    │   └── Zone maps updated with new min/max per block
    │   └── Columnar encoding: each column compressed separately
    │
    └── Verify: row counts match between Kafka offset and Redshift
    │
    ▼
TIME 5 min + 30s — Data available in Redshift
    │
    ▼
TIME next morning — Analyst runs cohort analysis query
    │
    ├── Redshift scans fact_orders across 2 years
    │   └── Zone maps: skip blocks where created_at outside range
    │   └── Columnar: reads ONLY customer_id, created_at, total columns
    │   └── MPP: each slice processes its portion in parallel
    │   └── Leader node merges results
    │
    └── Result: cohort retention table → displayed in QuickSight dashboard


INTERNAL STORAGE COMPARISON FOR THIS SINGLE ORDER:

┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ MONGODB (WiredTiger)  │ REDSHIFT (Columnar)                          │
├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ONE BSON document     │ 1 row in fact_orders                         │
│ ~2KB complete         │ + 2 rows in fact_order_items                 │
│                       │ + dim updates if new customer/product        │
│ Stored as contiguous  │ Each column value stored in separate block   │
│ binary blob in B-tree │                                              │
│ page                  │ order_id → block_order_id_001               │
│                       │ total → block_total_001                      │
│ ALL fields together   │ status → block_status_001                    │
│ → fast single-doc     │ created_at → block_created_at_001           │
│   retrieval           │                                              │
│                       │ Fields SEPARATED → fast column scan          │
│ Index on _id:         │ Zone map on created_at:                      │
│ B-tree → O(log n)     │ min/max per block → block skipping           │
│ lookup                │                                              │
│                       │ Distribution on customer_id:                 │
│ Shard key routes to   │ Same customer's orders on same slice         │
│ correct shard         │ → co-located JOIN with dim_customers         │
└──────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

📖 REDSHIFT-TO-MONGODB TRANSLATION GLOSSARY — Project 45

ARCHITECTURE-LEVEL TRANSLATIONS:

┌──────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ REDSHIFT ARCHITECTURE             │ MONGODB + REDSHIFT ARCHITECTURE         │
├──────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Single warehouse for everything   │ MongoDB for OLTP + Redshift for OLAP   │
│                                   │                                         │
│ ETL from application DB → Redshift│ Change Streams → Kafka → Redshift       │
│ (external CDC tool like DMS)      │ (native CDC, no external tool needed)   │
│                                   │                                         │
│ Materialized views for speed      │ MongoDB computed fields for real-time   │
│                                   │ Redshift MVs for analytical pre-compute │
│                                   │                                         │
│ Redshift Spectrum for data lake   │ Atlas Data Federation + Spectrum        │
│                                   │ Both query S3 data lake                 │
│                                   │                                         │
│ Concurrency scaling ($$)          │ MongoDB sharding (horizontal scale)     │
│                                   │ Redshift for analytical concurrency     │
│                                   │                                         │
│ QuickSight for all dashboards     │ MongoDB Charts for operational metrics  │
│                                   │ QuickSight for analytical dashboards    │
│                                   │                                         │
│ One schema, one truth             │ Two representations of same data:       │
│                                   │ → Denormalized in MongoDB (for app)     │
│                                   │ → Normalized in Redshift (for analysis) │
│                                   │ → Kafka ensures consistency             │
│                                   │                                         │
│ VPC + IAM for security            │ MongoDB RBAC + VPC Peering + IAM        │
│                                   │ Separate security domains per system    │
│                                   │                                         │
│ Single-region default             │ MongoDB global cluster + Redshift       │
│                                   │ in primary analytics region             │
└──────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┘

⚠️ COMMON PITFALLS AND FAILURE MODES

ARCHITECTURE-LEVEL MISTAKES:

PITFALL 1: "Let me query MongoDB for analytics"
    ❌ Analyst connects to MongoDB replica secondary
       Runs: db.orders.aggregate([{$group: ...}]) across 100M documents
       Result: secondary replication lag spikes, operational reads slow down
    ✅ Analytics queries go to Redshift ONLY
       MongoDB secondaries reserved for application read scaling
    WHY: MongoDB aggregation on large data = full document scan
         Redshift columnar scan is 10-100x more efficient for this

PITFALL 2: "Let me serve API from Redshift for some endpoints"
    ❌ "Product recommendations are precomputed in Redshift,
        let the app query Redshift directly"
       Result: WLM slot consumed per API call → 50K users → WLM exhausted
    ✅ Precompute in Redshift → push results back to MongoDB → serve from MongoDB
       COPY recommendations TO S3 → mongoimport → MongoDB serves API
    WHY: Redshift not designed for high-concurrency point lookups

PITFALL 3: "We don't need Kafka — just direct MongoDB → Redshift"
    ❌ Cron job: every 5 min, read MongoDB change stream, write to Redshift
       What happens when: Redshift is resizing? ETL job crashes? Network blip?
       Result: data loss, gaps in analytics, inconsistent reports
    ✅ MongoDB → Kafka (durable, replayable) → Redshift
       Kafka retains events for 7 days → replay on failure
       Multiple consumers → add ML pipeline without touching ETL
    WHY: Kafka decouples and buffers (Project 44)

PITFALL 4: "Same document model in both systems"
    ❌ Trying to load MongoDB's nested documents into Redshift as-is
       Using Redshift SUPER type to store JSON blobs
       Querying: SELECT json_extract(doc, '$.items[0].price') → terrible performance
    ✅ TRANSFORM documents into normalized star schema during ETL
       One nested MongoDB document → multiple normalized Redshift rows
       Each Redshift column is a proper typed column (DECIMAL, TIMESTAMP)
    WHY: Redshift's power comes from typed columnar storage
         JSON blobs in Redshift = worst of both worlds

PITFALL 5: "Global cluster means global Redshift too"
    ❌ Running Redshift clusters in US, EU, and ASIA
       Keeping them in sync with cross-region ETL
       Analysts confused about which cluster to query
    ✅ MongoDB global cluster for operational data (multi-region)
       ONE Redshift cluster in primary analytics region (usually US)
       Redshift data sharing for read replicas if needed
    WHY: Analytical workloads don't need <50ms latency
         One cluster = one source of truth for analytics
         MongoDB handles the geo-distribution requirement

PITFALL 6: "Let's start with Redshift and add MongoDB later"
    ❌ Building the app on Redshift → hitting concurrency limits → panic migration
       Data model deeply coupled to SQL JOINs → painful to restructure as documents
    ✅ Start with MongoDB for operational layer from day 1
       Add Redshift when analytical queries justify it
       Design the Kafka pipeline early (even if simple initially)
    WHY: Moving from document → table is easy (flatten)
         Moving from table → document requires rethinking access patterns

📈 SCALABILITY CONSIDERATIONS

SCALING THIS ARCHITECTURE AT 10x GROWTH:

CURRENT: 50K orders/hour, 15M users, 2M products
10x:     500K orders/hour, 150M users, 20M products (Black Friday scenario)

MONGODB SCALING:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ Component      │ Current         │ 10x Scale             │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ Products       │ M40 (3-node RS) │ Sharded (4 shards)    │
    │ Carts          │ M40 (3-node RS) │ Sharded (8 shards)    │
    │ Orders         │ M40 (3-node RS) │ Sharded (6 shards)    │
    │ Users          │ M40 (3-node RS) │ Sharded (4 shards)    │
    │ Inventory      │ M30 (3-node RS) │ Sharded (4 shards)    │
    │                │                 │                        │
    │ Atlas Search   │ S20 (2 nodes)   │ S80 (8 nodes)         │
    │                │                 │                        │
    │ Global Regions │ 3 (US/EU/ASIA)  │ 5 (add SA, AFRICA)    │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    
    HOW: add shards → data automatically rebalanced
         add regions → zone sharding extended
         NO downtime during scaling (live resharding — Project 40)

REDSHIFT SCALING:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ Component        │ Current          │ 10x Scale           │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ Cluster Type     │ RA3.xlplus       │ RA3.4xlarge          │
    │ Node Count       │ 4 nodes          │ 16 nodes             │
    │ Storage          │ 5TB              │ 50TB managed storage │
    │ Concurrency      │ 15 WLM slots     │ Concurrency scaling  │
    │ Spectrum         │ Clickstream only │ + raw orders archive │
    │                  │                  │ + ML feature store   │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    
    HOW: elastic resize (brief pause) or RA3 managed storage auto-scales
         move older data to Spectrum (S3) → infinite storage
         concurrency scaling adds transient clusters for burst

KAFKA SCALING:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │ Component        │ Current          │ 10x Scale           │
    ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    │ Brokers          │ 3                │ 12                   │
    │ Partitions/Topic │ 6                │ 24                   │
    │ Throughput       │ 50K msgs/hr      │ 500K msgs/hr         │
    │ Retention        │ 7 days           │ 14 days              │
    │ Consumer Groups  │ 4                │ 8+ (more services)   │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

💻 COMPLETE WORKING CODE — Infrastructure Setup

MongoDB Atlas Setup — pymongo

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PROJECT 45 — COMPLETE E-COMMERCE PLATFORM SETUP
# MongoDB operational layer initialization
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

from pymongo import MongoClient, ASCENDING, DESCENDING
from pymongo.write_concern import WriteConcern
from pymongo.read_concern import ReadConcern
from pymongo.read_preferences import ReadPreference
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from bson import ObjectId, Decimal128
import json

# ─── CONNECTION ───────────────────────────────────────
client = MongoClient(
    "mongodb+srv://admin:pass@novamart-global.mongodb.net/",
    w="majority",
    readPreference="nearest",
    retryWrites=True
)
db = client["ecommerce"]

# ─── COLLECTION SETUP WITH VALIDATION ─────────────────

# Products collection with schema validation
db.create_collection("products", validator={
    "$jsonSchema": {
        "bsonType": "object",
        "required": ["sku", "name", "price", "status"],
        "properties": {
            "sku":    {"bsonType": "string"},
            "name":   {"bsonType": "string"},
            "status": {"enum": ["active", "draft", "archived"]},
            "price": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "required": ["amount", "currency"],
                "properties": {
                    "amount":   {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                    "currency": {"bsonType": "string", "minLength": 3, "maxLength": 3}
                }
            }
        }
    }
})

# Orders collection with validation
db.create_collection("orders", validator={
    "$jsonSchema": {
        "bsonType": "object",
        "required": ["order_number", "customer", "items", "pricing", "status"],
        "properties": {
            "order_number": {"bsonType": "string"},
            "customer": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "required": ["user_id"],
                "properties": {
                    "user_id": {"bsonType": "objectId"}
                }
            },
            "items": {
                "bsonType": "array",
                "minItems": 1,
                "items": {
                    "bsonType": "object",
                    "required": ["product_id", "sku", "price", "quantity"],
                    "properties": {
                        "product_id": {"bsonType": "objectId"},
                        "sku":        {"bsonType": "string"},
                        "price":      {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                        "quantity":   {"bsonType": "int", "minimum": 1}
                    }
                }
            },
            "pricing": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "required": ["subtotal", "total"],
                "properties": {
                    "subtotal": {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                    "discount": {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                    "tax":      {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                    "shipping": {"bsonType": "decimal"},
                    "total":    {"bsonType": "decimal"}
                }
            },
            "status": {
                "enum": [
                    "created", "paid", "processing",
                    "shipped", "delivered", "cancelled", "refunded"
                ]
            },
            "region": {"enum": ["US", "EU", "ASIA"]}
        }
    }
})

# Carts collection with validation
db.create_collection("carts", validator={
    "$jsonSchema": {
        "bsonType": "object",
        "required": ["status", "items"],
        "properties": {
            "status": {"enum": ["active", "checkout", "converted", "abandoned"]},
            "items":  {"bsonType": "array"},
            "subtotal": {"bsonType": "decimal"}
        }
    }
})

# Users collection with validation
db.create_collection("users", validator={
    "$jsonSchema": {
        "bsonType": "object",
        "required": ["auth", "profile"],
        "properties": {
            "auth": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "required": ["email", "password_hash"],
                "properties": {
                    "email":         {"bsonType": "string"},
                    "password_hash": {"bsonType": "string"}
                }
            },
            "profile": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "required": ["first_name", "last_name"],
                "properties": {
                    "first_name": {"bsonType": "string"},
                    "last_name":  {"bsonType": "string"}
                }
            },
            "data_governance": {
                "bsonType": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "data_region": {"enum": ["US", "EU", "ASIA"]}
                }
            }
        }
    }
})

# Inventory collection
db.create_collection("inventory")

# Reviews collection (separate from products — referenced)
db.create_collection("reviews")

# Notifications collection
db.create_collection("notifications")

print("All collections created with validation schemas")


# ─── INDEX CREATION ───────────────────────────────────

def create_all_indexes():
    """
    Complete index strategy for all collections
    References: Projects 9-11 (indexing)
    """
    
    # ── PRODUCTS ──────────────────────────────────────
    db.products.create_index([("sku", ASCENDING)], unique=True, name="idx_sku_unique")
    db.products.create_index([("slug", ASCENDING)], unique=True, name="idx_slug_unique")
    db.products.create_index(
        [("category", ASCENDING), ("computed.avg_rating", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_category_rating"
    )
    db.products.create_index(
        [("brand", ASCENDING), ("status", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_brand_status"
    )
    db.products.create_index([("price.amount", ASCENDING)], name="idx_price")
    db.products.create_index(
        [("status", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_status_created"
    )
    db.products.create_index(
        [("inventory.total_available", ASCENDING)],
        partialFilterExpression={"status": "active"},
        name="idx_active_inventory_partial"
    )
    print("  ✓ Products indexes created (7 indexes)")
    
    # ── CARTS ─────────────────────────────────────────
    db.carts.create_index([("user_id", ASCENDING)], sparse=True, name="idx_user_id")
    db.carts.create_index([("session_id", ASCENDING)], sparse=True, name="idx_session_id")
    db.carts.create_index(
        [("expires_at", ASCENDING)],
        expireAfterSeconds=0,
        name="idx_ttl_expires"
    )
    db.carts.create_index(
        [("status", ASCENDING), ("updated_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_status_updated"
    )
    print("  ✓ Carts indexes created (4 indexes)")
    
    # ── ORDERS ────────────────────────────────────────
    db.orders.create_index(
        [("order_number", ASCENDING)],
        unique=True,
        name="idx_order_number_unique"
    )
    db.orders.create_index(
        [("customer.user_id", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_customer_orders"
    )
    db.orders.create_index(
        [("status", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_status_created"
    )
    db.orders.create_index(
        [("items.product_id", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_item_product"
    )
    db.orders.create_index(
        [("region", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_region_created"
    )
    print("  ✓ Orders indexes created (5 indexes)")
    
    # ── USERS ─────────────────────────────────────────
    db.users.create_index(
        [("auth.email", ASCENDING)],
        unique=True,
        name="idx_email_unique"
    )
    db.users.create_index(
        [("membership.tier", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_tier"
    )
    db.users.create_index(
        [("data_governance.data_region", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_data_region"
    )
    db.users.create_index(
        [("stats.last_order_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_last_order"
    )
    print("  ✓ Users indexes created (4 indexes)")
    
    # ── INVENTORY ─────────────────────────────────────
    db.inventory.create_index(
        [("product_id", ASCENDING), ("warehouse_id", ASCENDING)],
        unique=True,
        name="idx_product_warehouse_unique"
    )
    db.inventory.create_index(
        [("sku", ASCENDING), ("warehouse_id", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_sku_warehouse"
    )
    db.inventory.create_index(
        [("quantity.available", ASCENDING)],
        partialFilterExpression={"quantity.available": {"$lt": 100}},
        name="idx_low_stock_partial"
    )
    print("  ✓ Inventory indexes created (3 indexes)")
    
    # ── REVIEWS ───────────────────────────────────────
    db.reviews.create_index(
        [("product_id", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_product_reviews"
    )
    db.reviews.create_index(
        [("user_id", ASCENDING), ("created_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_user_reviews"
    )
    db.reviews.create_index(
        [("product_id", ASCENDING), ("rating", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_product_rating"
    )
    print("  ✓ Reviews indexes created (3 indexes)")
    
    # ── NOTIFICATIONS ─────────────────────────────────
    db.notifications.create_index(
        [("expires_at", ASCENDING)],
        expireAfterSeconds=0,
        name="idx_ttl_expires"
    )
    db.notifications.create_index(
        [("user_id", ASCENDING), ("sent_at", DESCENDING)],
        name="idx_user_notifications"
    )
    db.notifications.create_index(
        [("status", ASCENDING), ("scheduled_at", ASCENDING)],
        name="idx_processing_queue"
    )
    print("  ✓ Notifications indexes created (3 indexes)")

create_all_indexes()
print("\nTotal indexes created: 29 (+ 7 automatic _id indexes = 36)")


# ─── SEED DATA ────────────────────────────────────────

def seed_sample_data():
    """
    Insert realistic sample data for all collections
    """
    
    # ── Users ─────────────────────────────────────────
    users = [
        {
            "_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001"),
            "auth": {
                "email": "sarah.johnson@example.com",
                "password_hash": "[REDACTED:PASSWORD]",
                "mfa_enabled": True,
                "last_login": datetime.now()
            },
            "profile": {
                "first_name": "Sarah",
                "last_name": "Johnson",
                "display_name": "Sarah J.",
                "locale": "en-US",
                "timezone": "America/Chicago"
            },
            "addresses": [
                {
                    "_id": ObjectId(),
                    "label": "Home",
                    "street": "123 Oak Lane",
                    "city": "Austin",
                    "state": "TX",
                    "zip": "78701",
                    "country": "US",
                    "is_default_shipping": True,
                    "is_default_billing": True
                }
            ],
            "membership": {
                "tier": "Gold",
                "points": 15820,
                "tier_qualified_at": datetime(2024, 1, 15)
            },
            "stats": {
                "total_orders": 47,
                "total_spent": 4823.50,
                "avg_order_value": 102.63,
                "last_order_at": datetime(2024, 5, 28),
                "first_order_at": datetime(2023, 1, 15),
                "favorite_categories": ["Electronics", "Books"],
                "favorite_brands": ["TechCo"]
            },
            "preferences": {
                "notifications": {"email_marketing": True, "push_order_updates": True},
                "display": {"currency": "USD", "language": "en"}
            },
            "data_governance": {
                "data_region": "US",
                "consent_marketing": {"granted": True, "at": datetime(2023, 1, 15)}
            },
            "created_at": datetime(2023, 1, 15),
            "updated_at": datetime.now()
        },
        {
            "_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa002"),
            "auth": {
                "email": "hans.mueller@example.de",
                "password_hash": "$2b$12$XYZabc123def456ghi789jkl",
                "mfa_enabled": False,
                "last_login": datetime.now()
            },
            "profile": {
                "first_name": "Hans",
                "last_name": "Müller",
                "display_name": "Hans M.",
                "locale": "de-DE",
                "timezone": "Europe/Berlin"
            },
            "addresses": [
                {
                    "_id": ObjectId(),
                    "label": "Zuhause",
                    "street": "Berliner Str. 42",
                    "city": "Munich",
                    "state": "Bavaria",
                    "zip": "80331",
                    "country": "DE",
                    "is_default_shipping": True,
                    "is_default_billing": True
                }
            ],
            "membership": {"tier": "Silver", "points": 5200},
            "stats": {
                "total_orders": 12,
                "total_spent": 1580.00,
                "avg_order_value": 131.67,
                "last_order_at": datetime(2024, 5, 20),
                "first_order_at": datetime(2023, 8, 10),
                "favorite_categories": ["Footwear", "Electronics"],
                "favorite_brands": ["AthletePro"]
            },
            "data_governance": {
                "data_region": "EU",       # GDPR: stays on EU shards
                "consent_marketing": {"granted": True, "at": datetime(2023, 8, 10)}
            },
            "created_at": datetime(2023, 8, 10),
            "updated_at": datetime.now()
        }
    ]
    db.users.insert_many(users)
    print(f"  ✓ Inserted {len(users)} users")
    
    # ── Products ──────────────────────────────────────
    products = [
        {
            "_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb001"),
            "sku": "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
            "name": "ProGlide Wireless Mouse",
            "slug": "proglide-wireless-mouse",
            "brand": "TechCo",
            "category": ["Electronics", "Computer Accessories", "Mice"],
            "price": {
                "amount": Decimal128("29.99"),
                "currency": "USD",
                "compare_at": Decimal128("39.99"),
                "cost": Decimal128("12.50")
            },
            "status": "active",
            "computed": {
                "avg_rating": 4.3,
                "review_count": 1247,
                "total_sold": 15820,
                "view_count_30d": 45000
            },
            "recent_reviews": [
                {
                    "user_name": "Alice M.",
                    "rating": 5,
                    "title": "Best mouse ever",
                    "snippet": "Incredibly smooth tracking...",
                    "date": datetime(2024, 5, 28),
                    "helpful_votes": 42
                }
            ],
            "images": [
                {
                    "url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-main.jpg",
                    "alt": "Front view",
                    "primary": True
                }
            ],
            "attributes": {
                "connectivity": ["Bluetooth 5.0", "USB-C Dongle"],
                "dpi": {"min": 400, "max": 16000},
                "battery": {"type": "AA", "life_months": 18},
                "weight_grams": 85,
                "warranty_months": 24
            },
            "inventory": {
                "total_available": 1523,
                "warehouses": [
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-EAST", "qty": 450, "reserved": 12},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-WEST", "qty": 380, "reserved": 8},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-EU-CENTRAL", "qty": 400, "reserved": 5},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-ASIA-EAST", "qty": 293, "reserved": 3}
                ]
            },
            "search_keywords": ["wireless mouse", "bluetooth mouse", "ergonomic mouse"],
            "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 15),
            "updated_at": datetime.now()
        },
        {
            "_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb002"),
            "sku": "CLTH-RS-2024-001",
            "name": "TrailBlazer Running Shoes",
            "slug": "trailblazer-running-shoes",
            "brand": "AthletePro",
            "category": ["Footwear", "Running", "Men"],
            "price": {
                "amount": Decimal128("129.99"),
                "currency": "USD",
                "compare_at": None,
                "cost": Decimal128("45.00")
            },
            "status": "active",
            "computed": {
                "avg_rating": 4.7,
                "review_count": 3891,
                "total_sold": 28450,
                "view_count_30d": 92000
            },
            "recent_reviews": [
                {
                    "user_name": "Mike R.",
                    "rating": 5,
                    "title": "Perfect for daily runs",
                    "snippet": "Lightweight and responsive cushioning...",
                    "date": datetime(2024, 5, 25),
                    "helpful_votes": 38
                }
            ],
            "images": [
                {
                    "url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/rs001-main.jpg",
                    "alt": "Front view",
                    "primary": True
                }
            ],
            "attributes": {
                "gender": "Men",
                "color_options": [
                    {"name": "Midnight Black", "hex": "#1a1a2e", "available": True},
                    {"name": "Ocean Blue", "hex": "#0077b6", "available": True}
                ],
                "sizes": [
                    {"size": "9", "us": "9", "eu": "42", "in_stock": True},
                    {"size": "10", "us": "10", "eu": "43", "in_stock": True},
                    {"size": "11", "us": "11", "eu": "44", "in_stock": False}
                ],
                "material": "Mesh upper, EVA midsole, rubber outsole",
                "drop_mm": 8,
                "weight_grams": 280
            },
            "inventory": {
                "total_available": 4200,
                "warehouses": [
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-EAST", "qty": 1200, "reserved": 30},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-WEST", "qty": 1000, "reserved": 25},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-EU-CENTRAL", "qty": 1100, "reserved": 15},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-ASIA-EAST", "qty": 900, "reserved": 10}
                ]
            },
            "search_keywords": ["running shoes", "trail shoes", "athletic shoes"],
            "created_at": datetime(2024, 2, 10),
            "updated_at": datetime.now()
        },
        {
            "_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb003"),
            "sku": "BOOK-PCC-2024-001",
            "name": "Python Crash Course",
            "slug": "python-crash-course",
            "brand": "No Starch Press",
            "category": ["Books", "Programming", "Python"],
            "price": {
                "amount": Decimal128("35.99"),
                "currency": "USD",
                "compare_at": Decimal128("45.99"),
                "cost": Decimal128("8.00")
            },
            "status": "active",
            "computed": {
                "avg_rating": 4.8,
                "review_count": 8920,
                "total_sold": 125000,
                "view_count_30d": 180000
            },
            "recent_reviews": [],
            "images": [
                {
                    "url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/pcc-main.jpg",
                    "alt": "Book cover",
                    "primary": True
                }
            ],
            "attributes": {
                "isbn": "978-1593279288",
                "author": "Eric Matthes",
                "publisher": "No Starch Press",
                "pages": 544,
                "edition": 3,
                "format": "Paperback",
                "language": "English",
                "publication_date": datetime(2023, 1, 10)
            },
            "inventory": {
                "total_available": 8500,
                "warehouses": [
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-EAST", "qty": 3000, "reserved": 50},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-US-WEST", "qty": 2500, "reserved": 40},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-EU-CENTRAL", "qty": 2000, "reserved": 20},
                    {"warehouse_id": "WH-ASIA-EAST", "qty": 1000, "reserved": 10}
                ]
            },
            "search_keywords": ["python book", "programming book", "learn python",
                                "crash course", "beginner python"],
            "created_at": datetime(2023, 1, 10),
            "updated_at": datetime.now()
        }
    ]
    db.products.insert_many(products)
    print(f"  ✓ Inserted {len(products)} products (3 different types: electronics, footwear, book)")
    
    # ── Inventory (detailed per warehouse) ────────────
    inventory_docs = []
    for product in products:
        for wh in product["inventory"]["warehouses"]:
            inventory_docs.append({
                "product_id": product["_id"],
                "sku": product["sku"],
                "warehouse_id": wh["warehouse_id"],
                "quantity": {
                    "on_hand": wh["qty"] + wh["reserved"],
                    "reserved": wh["reserved"],
                    "available": wh["qty"],
                    "incoming": 0,
                    "damaged": 0
                },
                "thresholds": {
                    "reorder_point": 100,
                    "reorder_quantity": 500,
                    "safety_stock": 50,
                    "max_stock": 5000
                },
                "location": {
                    "zone": "A",
                    "aisle": str(hash(product["sku"]) % 20 + 1),
                    "shelf": "B",
                    "bin": "3"
                },
                "recent_movements": [],
                "updated_at": datetime.now()
            })
    db.inventory.insert_many(inventory_docs)
    print(f"  ✓ Inserted {len(inventory_docs)} inventory records")
    
    # ── Cart for Sarah ────────────────────────────────
    cart = {
        "_id": ObjectId("ccccccccccccccccccccc001"),
        "user_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001"),
        "session_id": "sess_abc123def456",
        "status": "active",
        "items": [
            {
                "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb001"),
                "sku": "ELEC-WM-2024-001",
                "name": "ProGlide Wireless Mouse",
                "image_url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/wm001-main.jpg",
                "price_at_add": Decimal128("29.99"),
                "current_price": Decimal128("29.99"),
                "quantity": 2,
                "variant": None,
                "added_at": datetime.now()
            },
            {
                "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb002"),
                "sku": "CLTH-RS-2024-001",
                "name": "TrailBlazer Running Shoes",
                "image_url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/rs001-main.jpg",
                "price_at_add": Decimal128("129.99"),
                "current_price": Decimal128("129.99"),
                "quantity": 1,
                "variant": {"size": "10", "color": "Midnight Black"},
                "added_at": datetime.now()
            }
        ],
        "subtotal": Decimal128("189.97"),
        "item_count": 3,
        "coupons": [],
        "created_at": datetime.now(),
        "updated_at": datetime.now(),
        "expires_at": datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)
    }
    db.carts.insert_one(cart)
    print(f"  ✓ Inserted 1 active cart")
    
    # ── Sample Reviews ────────────────────────────────
    reviews = [
        {
            "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb001"),
            "user_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001"),
            "rating": 5,
            "title": "Best mouse I've owned",
            "body": "The tracking is incredibly precise. Battery lasts forever. "
                    "Bluetooth connection is stable even at 10 feet from my laptop.",
            "verified_purchase": True,
            "helpful_votes": 42,
            "reported": False,
            "created_at": datetime(2024, 5, 28),
            "updated_at": datetime(2024, 5, 28)
        },
        {
            "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb001"),
            "user_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa002"),
            "rating": 4,
            "title": "Great mouse, minor scroll wheel issue",
            "body": "Very comfortable and accurate. The scroll wheel feels "
                    "slightly loose after 3 months but everything else is perfect.",
            "verified_purchase": True,
            "helpful_votes": 18,
            "reported": False,
            "created_at": datetime(2024, 5, 15),
            "updated_at": datetime(2024, 5, 15)
        },
        {
            "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb002"),
            "user_id": ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001"),
            "rating": 5,
            "title": "Perfect for daily 5K runs",
            "body": "Lightweight, responsive, and the cushioning is fantastic. "
                    "No blisters even on the first run. Highly recommend.",
            "verified_purchase": True,
            "helpful_votes": 67,
            "reported": False,
            "created_at": datetime(2024, 5, 20),
            "updated_at": datetime(2024, 5, 20)
        }
    ]
    db.reviews.insert_many(reviews)
    print(f"  ✓ Inserted {len(reviews)} reviews")

seed_sample_data()
print("\n✅ Seed data complete")


# ─── FULL PLATFORM OPERATIONS ─────────────────────────

def demo_product_page_api(product_slug):
    """
    Simulate product page API call
    ONE query returns EVERYTHING needed for product page
    
    In Redshift: this would be a 7-table JOIN (shown earlier)
    In MongoDB: this is ONE find() call
    """
    import time
    
    start = time.time()
    
    product = db.products.find_one(
        {"slug": product_slug, "status": "active"},
        {
            "name": 1,
            "brand": 1,
            "category": 1,
            "price": 1,
            "computed": 1,
            "recent_reviews": 1,
            "images": 1,
            "attributes": 1,
            "inventory.total_available": 1
        }
    )
    
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    
    if product:
        print(f"\n📦 PRODUCT PAGE: {product['name']}")
        print(f"   Brand: {product['brand']}")
        print(f"   Price: ${product['price']['amount']}")
        if product['price'].get('compare_at'):
            print(f"   Was: ${product['price']['compare_at']}")
        print(f"   Rating: {product['computed']['avg_rating']}★ "
              f"({product['computed']['review_count']} reviews)")
        print(f"   In Stock: {product['inventory']['total_available']} units")
        print(f"   Categories: {' > '.join(product['category'])}")
        print(f"\n   ⏱ Query time: {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms")
        print(f"   (Redshift equivalent: 200-1000ms for 7-table JOIN)")
    
    return product


def demo_cart_operations(cart_id):
    """
    Simulate cart operations
    Each operation is a single atomic update
    
    In Redshift: each would be BEGIN/UPDATE/UPDATE/COMMIT across 2 tables
    In MongoDB: each is ONE updateOne() call
    """
    import time
    
    print("\n🛒 CART OPERATIONS:")
    
    # ── Add item to cart ──
    start = time.time()
    result = db.carts.update_one(
        {
            "_id": cart_id,
            "status": "active",
            "items.product_id": {"$ne": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb003")}
        },
        {
            "$push": {
                "items": {
                    "product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb003"),
                    "sku": "BOOK-PCC-2024-001",
                    "name": "Python Crash Course",
                    "image_url": "https://cdn.novamart.com/img/pcc-main.jpg",
                    "price_at_add": Decimal128("35.99"),
                    "current_price": Decimal128("35.99"),
                    "quantity": 1,
                    "variant": None,
                    "added_at": datetime.now()
                }
            },
            "$inc": {"item_count": 1},
            "$set": {
                "subtotal": Decimal128("225.96"),
                "updated_at": datetime.now()
            }
        }
    )
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    print(f"   ✅ Added Python Crash Course → {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms "
          f"(modified: {result.modified_count})")
    
    # ── Update quantity ──
    start = time.time()
    result = db.carts.update_one(
        {
            "_id": cart_id,
            "items.product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb001")
        },
        {
            "$set": {
                "items.$.quantity": 3,
                "updated_at": datetime.now()
            },
            "$inc": {"item_count": 1}
        }
    )
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    print(f"   ✅ Updated mouse qty to 3 → {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms")
    
    # ── Remove item ──
    start = time.time()
    result = db.carts.update_one(
        {"_id": cart_id},
        {
            "$pull": {
                "items": {"product_id": ObjectId("bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb003")}
            },
            "$inc": {"item_count": -1},
            "$set": {
                "subtotal": Decimal128("219.96"),
                "updated_at": datetime.now()
            }
        }
    )
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    print(f"   ✅ Removed Python Crash Course → {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms")
    
    # ── View cart ──
    cart = db.carts.find_one({"_id": cart_id})
    print(f"\n   Cart contents ({cart['item_count']} items, ${cart['subtotal']}):")
    for item in cart["items"]:
        print(f"     • {item['name']} × {item['quantity']} @ ${item['price_at_add']}")


def demo_order_creation():
    """
    Full cart → order conversion with multi-document transaction
    Reference: Project 28
    """
    import time
    
    print("\n📋 ORDER CREATION (Multi-Document Transaction):")
    
    cart_id = ObjectId("ccccccccccccccccccccc001")
    user_id = ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001")
    
    start = time.time()
    
    with client.start_session() as session:
        with session.start_transaction(
            read_concern=ReadConcern("snapshot"),
            write_concern=WriteConcern("majority"),
            read_preference=ReadPreference.PRIMARY
        ):
            try:
                # Step 1: Read cart
                cart = db.carts.find_one(
                    {"_id": cart_id, "status": "active"},
                    session=session
                )
                if not cart:
                    raise Exception("Cart not found or inactive")
                
                # Step 2: Decrement inventory for each item
                for item in cart["items"]:
                    inv_result = db.inventory.update_one(
                        {
                            "product_id": item["product_id"],
                            "warehouse_id": "WH-US-EAST",
                            "quantity.available": {"$gte": item["quantity"]}
                        },
                        {
                            "$inc": {
                                "quantity.available": -item["quantity"],
                                "quantity.reserved": item["quantity"]
                            },
                            "$push": {
                                "recent_movements": {
                                    "$each": [{
                                        "type": "reservation",
                                        "quantity": -item["quantity"],
                                        "at": datetime.now(),
                                        "by": "order_service"
                                    }],
                                    "$slice": -10
                                }
                            },
                            "$set": {"updated_at": datetime.now()}
                        },
                        session=session
                    )
                    if inv_result.modified_count == 0:
                        raise Exception(f"Insufficient stock for {item['sku']}")
                
                # Step 3: Update product computed stats
                for item in cart["items"]:
                    db.products.update_one(
                        {"_id": item["product_id"]},
                        {
                            "$inc": {"computed.total_sold": item["quantity"]},
                            "$set": {"updated_at": datetime.now()}
                        },
                        session=session
                    )
                
                # Step 4: Create order document
                order_number = f"NM-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d')}-{str(ObjectId())[-6:]}"
                
                user = db.users.find_one(
                    {"_id": user_id},
                    {"profile": 1, "auth.email": 1, "membership.tier": 1},
                    session=session
                )
                
                order = {
                    "order_number": order_number,
                    "customer": {
                        "user_id": user_id,
                        "name": f"{user['profile']['first_name']} {user['profile']['last_name']}",
                        "email": user["auth"]["email"],
                        "tier": user["membership"]["tier"]
                    },
                    "items": [
                        {
                            "product_id": item["product_id"],
                            "sku": item["sku"],
                            "name": item["name"],
                            "price": item["price_at_add"],
                            "quantity": item["quantity"],
                            "subtotal": Decimal128(
                                str(float(str(item["price_at_add"])) * item["quantity"])
                            ),
                            "variant": item.get("variant"),
                            "warehouse_id": "WH-US-EAST"
                        }
                        for item in cart["items"]
                    ],
                    "pricing": {
                        "subtotal": cart["subtotal"],
                        "discount": Decimal128("0.00"),
                        "tax": Decimal128("15.37"),
                        "shipping": Decimal128("0.00"),
                        "total": Decimal128(
                            str(float(str(cart["subtotal"])) + 15.37)
                        )
                    },
                    "shipping": {
                        "address": {
                            "street": "123 Oak Lane",
                            "city": "Austin",
                            "state": "TX",
                            "zip": "78701",
                            "country": "US"
                        },
                        "method": "standard",
                        "estimated_delivery": datetime.now() + timedelta(days=5),
                        "tracking": None
                    },
                    "payment": {
                        "method": "credit_card",
                        "card_brand": "Visa",
                        "card_last_four": "[REDACTED:CREDIT_DEBIT_NUMBER]",
                        "transaction_id": f"txn_{str(ObjectId())}",
                        "status": "captured",
                        "captured_at": datetime.now()
                    },
                    "status": "paid",
                    "status_history": [
                        {"status": "created", "at": datetime.now(), "by": "system"},
                        {"status": "paid", "at": datetime.now(), "by": "payment_service"}
                    ],
                    "coupons_applied": [],
                    "source": "web",
                    "region": "US",
                    "created_at": datetime.now(),
                    "updated_at": datetime.now()
                }
                
                order_result = db.orders.insert_one(order, session=session)
                
                # Step 5: Mark cart as converted
                db.carts.update_one(
                    {"_id": cart_id},
                    {
                        "$set": {
                            "status": "converted",
                            "converted_to_order": order_result.inserted_id,
                            "updated_at": datetime.now()
                        }
                    },
                    session=session
                )
                
                # Step 6: Update user stats
                db.users.update_one(
                    {"_id": user_id},
                    {
                        "$inc": {
                            "stats.total_orders": 1,
                            "stats.total_spent": float(str(cart["subtotal"])),
                            "membership.points": int(float(str(cart["subtotal"])) * 10)
                        },
                        "$set": {
                            "stats.last_order_at": datetime.now(),
                            "updated_at": datetime.now()
                        }
                    },
                    session=session
                )
                
                # COMMIT
                elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
                
                print(f"   ✅ Order {order_number} created successfully")
                print(f"   ✅ Inventory decremented for {len(cart['items'])} items")
                print(f"   ✅ Cart marked as converted")
                print(f"   ✅ User stats updated (+1 order, +{cart['subtotal']} spent)")
                print(f"   ✅ Loyalty points awarded: +{int(float(str(cart['subtotal'])) * 10)}")
                print(f"\n   ⏱ Total transaction time: {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms")
                print(f"   (6 collections modified atomically)")
                print(f"   (Redshift equivalent: 500-2000ms across 6+ tables)")
                
                return order_result.inserted_id
                
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"   ❌ Transaction aborted: {e}")
                raise


def demo_order_history(user_id):
    """
    Fetch user's order history
    ONE query with embedded items — no JOINs needed
    """
    import time
    
    print(f"\n📜 ORDER HISTORY for user {user_id}:")
    
    start = time.time()
    
    orders = list(db.orders.find(
        {"customer.user_id": user_id},
        {
            "order_number": 1,
            "status": 1,
            "pricing.total": 1,
            "items.name": 1,
            "items.quantity": 1,
            "created_at": 1
        }
    ).sort("created_at", DESCENDING).limit(10))
    
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    
    for order in orders:
        items_summary = ", ".join(
            [f"{i['name']} ×{i['quantity']}" for i in order["items"]]
        )
        print(f"   {order['order_number']} | {order['status']} | "
              f"${order['pricing']['total']} | {items_summary}")
    
    print(f"\n   ⏱ Query time: {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms for {len(orders)} orders")
    print(f"   (Redshift: JOIN orders + order_items → 200-500ms)")


def demo_operational_analytics():
    """
    Real-time operational metrics from MongoDB
    For live dashboards (MongoDB Charts — Project 42)
    NOT for historical analytics (that's Redshift)
    """
    import time
    
    print("\n📊 REAL-TIME OPERATIONAL METRICS (MongoDB):")
    
    start = time.time()
    
    pipeline = [
        # Orders in the last 24 hours
        {
            "$match": {
                "created_at": {"$gte": datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24)}
            }
        },
        {
            "$facet": {
                # Total orders and revenue
                "summary": [
                    {
                        "$group": {
                            "_id": None,
                            "total_orders": {"$sum": 1},
                            "total_revenue": {"$sum": {"$toDouble": "$pricing.total"}},
                            "avg_order_value": {"$avg": {"$toDouble": "$pricing.total"}}
                        }
                    }
                ],
                # Orders by status
                "by_status": [
                    {
                        "$group": {
                            "_id": "$status",
                            "count": {"$sum": 1}
                        }
                    },
                    {"$sort": {"count": -1}}
                ],
                # Orders by region
                "by_region": [
                    {
                        "$group": {
                            "_id": "$region",
                            "count": {"$sum": 1},
                            "revenue": {"$sum": {"$toDouble": "$pricing.total"}}
                        }
                    },
                    {"$sort": {"revenue": -1}}
                ],
                # Top products (last 24h)
                "top_products": [
                    {"$unwind": "$items"},
                    {
                        "$group": {
                            "_id": "$items.sku",
                            "name": {"$first": "$items.name"},
                            "units_sold": {"$sum": "$items.quantity"},
                            "revenue": {
                                "$sum": {
                                    "$multiply": [
                                        {"$toDouble": "$items.price"},
                                        "$items.quantity"
                                    ]
                                }
                            }
                        }
                    },
                    {"$sort": {"revenue": -1}},
                    {"$limit": 5}
                ]
            }
        }
    ]
    
    result = list(db.orders.aggregate(pipeline))
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    
    if result:
        data = result[0]
        
        if data["summary"]:
            s = data["summary"][0]
            print(f"   Last 24h Summary:")
            print(f"     Orders: {s['total_orders']}")
            print(f"     Revenue: ${s['total_revenue']:,.2f}")
            print(f"     Avg Order Value: ${s['avg_order_value']:,.2f}")
        
        if data["by_status"]:
            print(f"\n   By Status:")
            for s in data["by_status"]:
                print(f"     {s['_id']}: {s['count']}")
        
        if data["by_region"]:
            print(f"\n   By Region:")
            for r in data["by_region"]:
                print(f"     {r['_id']}: {r['count']} orders, ${r['revenue']:,.2f}")
        
        if data["top_products"]:
            print(f"\n   Top Products:")
            for p in data["top_products"]:
                print(f"     {p['name']}: {p['units_sold']} units, ${p['revenue']:,.2f}")
    
    print(f"\n   ⏱ Query time: {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms")
    print(f"   (This is OPERATIONAL analytics — for real-time dashboards)")
    print(f"   (For historical analytics across years → use Redshift)")


def demo_low_stock_check():
    """
    Real-time inventory monitoring
    Partial index makes this extremely efficient
    """
    import time
    
    print("\n📦 LOW STOCK ALERTS:")
    
    start = time.time()
    
    low_stock = list(db.inventory.find(
        {"quantity.available": {"$lt": 100}},
        {
            "sku": 1,
            "warehouse_id": 1,
            "quantity.available": 1,
            "thresholds.reorder_point": 1
        }
    ).sort("quantity.available", ASCENDING))
    
    elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    
    if low_stock:
        for item in low_stock:
            print(f"   ⚠️  {item['sku']} @ {item['warehouse_id']}: "
                  f"{item['quantity']['available']} available "
                  f"(reorder point: {item['thresholds']['reorder_point']})")
    else:
        print(f"   ✅ All inventory levels healthy")
    
    print(f"\n   ⏱ Query time: {elapsed_ms:.2f}ms (using partial index)")


# ─── RUN ALL DEMOS ────────────────────────────────────

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("  NOVAMART E-COMMERCE PLATFORM — FULL DEMO")
print("=" * 70)

# 1. Product page
demo_product_page_api("proglide-wireless-mouse")

# 2. Cart operations
demo_cart_operations(ObjectId("ccccccccccccccccccccc001"))

# 3. Order creation (multi-document transaction)
order_id = demo_order_creation()

# 4. Order history
demo_order_history(ObjectId("aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa001"))

# 5. Operational analytics
demo_operational_analytics()

# 6. Inventory monitoring
demo_low_stock_check()

# 7. Platform health summary
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("  PLATFORM HEALTH SUMMARY")
print("=" * 70)
for coll_name in db.list_collection_names():
    count = db[coll_name].count_documents({})
    indexes = len(list(db[coll_name].list_indexes()))
    print(f"   {coll_name:20s}: {count:6d} documents, {indexes:2d} indexes")

# Cleanup connection
client.close()
print("\n✅ Demo complete. Connection closed.")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EQUIVALENT REDSHIFT SQL — For the same demo operations
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# -- 1. Product page (7-table JOIN)
# SELECT p.*, pa.attr_key, pa.attr_value,
#        r_agg.avg_rating, r_agg.review_count,
#        pi.url, pi.alt_text,
#        inv.warehouse_id, inv.qty
# FROM store.products p
# LEFT JOIN store.product_attributes pa ON p.product_id = pa.product_id
# LEFT JOIN (SELECT product_id, AVG(rating) avg_rating, COUNT(*) review_count
#            FROM store.reviews GROUP BY 1) r_agg ON p.product_id = r_agg.product_id
# LEFT JOIN store.product_images pi ON p.product_id = pi.product_id
# LEFT JOIN store.inventory inv ON p.product_id = inv.product_id
# WHERE p.slug = 'proglide-wireless-mouse' AND p.status = 'active';
# -- Latency: 200-1000ms | Concurrency: limited by WLM
#
# -- 2. Cart add item (2-table transaction)
# BEGIN;
# INSERT INTO store.cart_items (cart_id, product_id, sku, name, price, qty)
# VALUES ('cart_001', 'prod_003', 'BOOK-PCC-2024-001', 'Python Crash Course', 35.99, 1);
# UPDATE store.carts SET subtotal = subtotal + 35.99, item_count = item_count + 1,
#        updated_at = GETDATE() WHERE cart_id = 'cart_001';
# COMMIT;
# -- Latency: 50-200ms per operation | At 50K concurrent: WLM exhausted
#
# -- 3. Order creation (6-table transaction)
# -- Redshift doesn't support FOR UPDATE (no row locking)
# -- Concurrent orders would serialize at table level
# -- Not practical for 50K orders/hour
#
# -- 4. Order history (2-table JOIN)
# SELECT o.order_number, o.status, o.total, o.created_at,
#        oi.product_name, oi.quantity
# FROM store.orders o
# JOIN store.order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
# WHERE o.customer_id = 'user_001'
# ORDER BY o.created_at DESC LIMIT 10;
# -- Latency: 100-500ms (DISTKEY on customer_id helps)
#
# -- 5. Operational analytics (Redshift CAN do this but slowly)
# SELECT COUNT(*) total_orders, SUM(total) revenue, AVG(total) aov
# FROM store.orders WHERE created_at >= GETDATE() - INTERVAL '24 hours';
# -- Latency: 2-10s (full scan even with sort key on created_at)
# -- For HISTORICAL analytics across years: Redshift excels
# -- For REAL-TIME operational metrics: MongoDB is 100x faster
#
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📊 COMPLETE ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY DIAGRAM

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        NOVAMART DATA PLATFORM                                │
│                     Complete Architecture Map                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  ┌─── USER-FACING LAYER ──────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │  Web App │ Mobile App │ Partner API │ Admin Dashboard               │     │
│  └──────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                              │                                               │
│  ┌─── API LAYER ────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │  API Gateway (Kong)       │                                         │     │
│  │  ├── Product Service ─────┤                                         │     │
│  │  ├── Cart Service ────────┤                                         │     │
│  │  ├── Order Service ───────┤                                         │     │
│  │  ├── User Service ────────┤                                         │     │
│  │  ├── Search Service ──────┤                                         │     │
│  │  ├── Inventory Service ───┤                                         │     │
│  │  └── Notification Service ┤                                         │     │
│  └───────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                              │                                               │
│  ┌─── OPERATIONAL LAYER ─────┼──── MongoDB Atlas Global Cluster ──────┐     │
│  │                           │                                         │     │
│  │  ┌─── US Region ──────────┴──────────────────┐                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 1: products (hashed _id)           │                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 2: orders (region=US + customer_id) │                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 3: users (region=US + _id)          │                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 4: carts (hashed user_id)           │                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 5: inventory (warehouse_id)         │                     │     │
│  │  │  + Atlas Search nodes (product search)     │                     │     │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘                     │     │
│  │                                                                      │     │
│  │  ┌─── EU Region ─────────────────────────────┐                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 6: orders (region=EU + customer_id) │ ← GDPR compliant  │     │
│  │  │  Shard 7: users (region=EU + _id)          │ ← data stays here │     │
│  │  │  + Read replicas for products/inventory    │                     │     │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘                     │     │
│  │                                                                      │     │
│  │  ┌─── ASIA Region ───────────────────────────┐                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 8: orders (region=ASIA)             │                     │     │
│  │  │  Shard 9: users (region=ASIA)              │                     │     │
│  │  │  + Read replicas for products/inventory    │                     │     │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘                     │     │
│  │                                                                      │     │
│  │  Change Streams active on: orders, products, users, inventory       │     │
│  └──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                              │                                               │
│  ┌─── EVENT BACKBONE ───────┼──── Apache Kafka (MSK) ─────────────────┐     │
│  │                           │                                         │     │
│  │  MongoDB Kafka Source Connector                                     │     │
│  │  ├── novamart.ecommerce.orders    → 50K msgs/hr                    │     │
│  │  ├── novamart.ecommerce.products  → 5K msgs/hr                     │     │
│  │  ├── novamart.ecommerce.users     → 10K msgs/hr                    │     │
│  │  ├── novamart.ecommerce.inventory → 100K msgs/hr                   │     │
│  │  └── novamart.clickstream         → 500K msgs/hr (from app)        │     │
│  │                                                                      │     │
│  │  Consumer Groups:                                                   │     │
│  │  ├── redshift-sink ──────────────────────────────────────┐          │     │
│  │  ├── s3-archive ─────────────────────────────┐           │          │     │
│  │  ├── fraud-detector ──────────────┐          │           │          │     │
│  │  ├── recommendation-engine ───┐   │          │           │          │     │
│  │  └── notification-service ─┐  │   │          │           │          │     │
│  └────────────────────────────┼──┼───┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┘     │
│                               │  │   │          │           │                │
│  ┌─── PROCESSING LAYER ──────┼──┼───┼──────────┼───────────┼────────────┐   │
│  │                            │  │   │          │           │            │   │
│  │  ┌── Notifications ───────┘  │   │          │           │            │   │
│  │  │  MongoDB notifications     │   │          │           │            │   │
│  │  │  collection + SendGrid     │   │          │           │            │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────┘   │          │           │            │   │
│  │                                    │          │           │            │   │
│  │  ┌── ML Pipeline (SageMaker) ─────┘          │           │            │   │
│  │  │  Fraud scoring (<200ms)                    │           │            │   │
│  │  │  Recommendations (nightly batch)           │           │            │   │
│  │  │  Results → MongoDB user.recommendations    │           │            │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘           │            │   │
│  │                                                            │            │   │
│  │  ┌── S3 Data Lake ────────────────────────────────────────┘            │   │
│  │  │  s3://novamart-data-lake/                                           │   │
│  │  │  ├── raw/orders/2024/05/31/         (JSON)                          │   │
│  │  │  ├── raw/clickstream/2024/05/31/    (JSON)                          │   │
│  │  │  ├── processed/orders/              (Parquet)                       │   │
│  │  │  ├── processed/clickstream/         (Parquet)                       │   │
│  │  │  └── ml/features/                   (Parquet)                       │   │
│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘            │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                            │                 │
│  ┌─── ANALYTICAL LAYER ───────────────────────────────────┼─────────────┐   │
│  │                                                         │             │   │
│  │  ┌── Amazon Redshift (RA3, 4-node) ────────────────────┘             │   │
│  │  │                                                                    │   │
│  │  │  Local Tables (star schema):                                      │   │
│  │  │  ├── analytics.dim_customers    (15M rows, DISTKEY customer_id)   │   │
